text.DRB: Thus saith the king: Let not Ezechias deceive you, for he shall not be able to deliver you.

text.NRSV: Do not let Hezekiah make you rely on the Lord by saying, The Lord will surely deliver us; this city will not be given into the hand of the king of Assyria.’
text.Rotherham: Neither let Hezekiah cause you to trust in Yahweh saying, Yahweh, will certainly deliver, us,—this city shall not be given over, into the hand of the king of Assyria.
text.DRB: And let not Ezechias make you trust in the Lord, saying: The Lord will surely deliver us, and this city shall not be given into the hands of the king of the Assyrians.

text.NRSV: Do not listen to Hezekiah; for thus says the king of Assyria: ‘Make your peace with me and come out to me; then everyone of you will eat from your own vine and your own fig tree and drink water from your own cistern,
text.Rotherham: Do not hearken unto Hezekiah,—for, thus, saith the king of Assyria, Deal with me thankfully, and come out unto me, Then shall ye eat, Every one of his own vine and Every one of his own fig-tree, And drink every one the Waters of his own cistern:
text.DRB: Do not hearken to Ezechias: for thus said the king of the Assyrians: Do with me that which is for your advantage, and come out to me, and eat ye every one of his vine, and every one of his fig tree, and drink ye every one the water of his cistern,

text.NRSV: until I come and take you away to a land like your own land, a land of grain and wine, a land of bread and vineyards.
text.Rotherham: Until I come and take you, into A land like your own land,—A land of corn and new wine, A land of bread and vineyards:—
text.DRB: Till I come and take you away to a land, like to your own, a land of corn and of wine, a land of bread and vineyards.

text.NRSV: Beware that Hezekiah does not mislead you by saying, The Lord will deliver us. Has any of the gods of the nations delivered their land out of the hand of the king of Assyria?
text.Rotherham: Lest Hezekiah, persuade you, saying, Yahweh will deliver us! Have the gods of the nations, delivered,—any one of them—his country, out of the hand of the king of Assyria?
text.DRB: Neither let Ezechias trouble you, saying: The Lord will deliver us. Have any of the gods of the nations delivered their land out of the hand of the king of the Assyrians?

text.NRSV: Where are the gods of Hamath and Arpad? Where are the gods of Sepharvaim? Have they delivered Samaria out of my hand?
text.Rotherham: Where are the gods of Hamath and Arpad? Where are the gods of Sepharvaim? If indeed they had only delivered Samaria, out of my hand!
text.DRB: Where is the god of Emath and of Arphad? where is the god of Sepharvaim? have they delivered Samaria out of my hand?

text.NRSV: Who among all the gods of these countries have delivered their countries out of my hand, that the Lord should deliver Jerusalem out of my hand?’ ”
text.Rotherham: Who are they, among all the gods of these countries that have delivered their country out of my hand? That, Yahweh, should deliver, Jerusalem, out of my hand!
text.DRB: Who is there among all the gods of these lands, that hath delivered his country out of my hand, that the Lord may deliver Jerusalem out of my hand?

text.NRSV: But they were silent and answered him not a word, for the king’s command was, “Do not answer him.”
text.Rotherham: But they held their peace, and answered him not a word,—for the command of the king, it was, saying,—Ye must not answer him.
text.DRB: And they held their peace, and answered him not a word. For the king had commanded, saying: answer him not.

text.NRSV: Then Eliakim son of Hilkiah, who was in charge of the palace, and Shebna the secretary, and Joah son of Asaph, the recorder, came to Hezekiah with their clothes torn, and told him the words of the Rabshakeh.
text.Rotherham: Then came in—Eliakim son of Hilkiah who was over the household, and Shebna the scribe, and Joah son of Asaph, the recorder, unto Hezekiah, with rent clothes,—and they told him the words of Rabshakeh.
text.DRB: And Eliacim the son of Helcias, that was over the house, and Sobna the scribe, and Joahe the son of Asaph the recorder, went in to Ezechias with their garments rent, and told him the words of Rabsaces.

text.NRSV: When King Hezekiah heard it, he tore his clothes, covered himself with sackcloth, and went into the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when King Hezekiah heard it, that he rent his clothes,—and covered himself with sackcloth, and entered the house of Yahweh;
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when king Ezechias had heard it, that he rent his garments and covered himself with sackcloth, and went into the house of the Lord.

text.NRSV: And he sent Eliakim, who was in charge of the palace, and Shebna the secretary, and the senior priests, covered with sackcloth, to the prophet Isaiah son of Amoz.
text.Rotherham: and sent Eliakim who was over the household and Shebna the scribe and the elders of the priests, covered with sackcloth,—unto Isaiah the prophet, Son of Amoz;
text.DRB: And he sent Eliacim who was over the house, and Sobna the scribe, and the ancients of the priests covered with sackcloth, to Isaias the son of Amos the prophet.

text.NRSV: They said to him, “Thus says Hezekiah, This day is a day of distress, of rebuke, and of disgrace; children have come to the birth, and there is no strength to bring them forth.
text.Rotherham: and they said unto him, Thus, saith Hezekiah, A day of distress and rebuke and reviling, is this day,—For children are come to the birth, and, strength, is there none to bring forth.
text.DRB: And they said to him: Thus saith Ezechias: This day is a day of tribulation, and of rebuke, and of blasphemy: for the children are come to the birth, and there is not strength to bring forth.

text.NRSV: It may be that the Lord your God heard the words of the Rabshakeh, whom his master the king of Assyria has sent to mock the living God, and will rebuke the words that the Lord your God has heard; therefore lift up your prayer for the remnant that is left.”
text.Rotherham: It may be, that Yahweh thy God will hear the words of Rabshakeh whom the king of Assyria his lord, hath sent to reproach a Living God, and will rebuke the words which Yahweh thy God hath heard,—Wherefore lift thou up a prayer, for the remnant that remaineth.
text.DRB: It may be the Lord thy God will hear the words of Rabsaces, whom the king of the Assyrians his master hath sent to blaspheme the living God, and to reproach with words which the Lord thy God hath heard: wherefore lift up thy prayer for the remnant that is left.

text.NRSV: When the servants of King Hezekiah came to Isaiah,
text.Rotherham: So the servants of King Hezekiah came unto Isaiah.
text.DRB: And the servants of Ezechias came to Isaias.

text.NRSV: Isaiah said to them, “Say to your master, ‘Thus says the Lord: Do not be afraid because of the words that you have heard, with which the servants of the king of Assyria have reviled me.
text.Rotherham: And Isaiah said unto them, Thus, shall ye, surely say, unto your lord,—Thus, saith Yahweh—Be not thou afraid because of the words which thou hast heard, wherewith the servants of the king of Assyria have reviled, Me.
text.DRB: And Isaias said to them: Thus shall you say to your master: Thus saith the Lord: Be not afraid of the words that thou hast heard, with which the servants of the king of the Assyrians have blasphemed me.

text.NRSV: I myself will put a spirit in him, so that he shall hear a rumor, and return to his own land; I will cause him to fall by the sword in his own land.’ ”
text.Rotherham: Behold me! about to let go against him a blast of alarm, and when he heareth the report, then will he return to his own country,—and I will cause him to fall by the sword in his own land.
text.DRB: Behold, I will send a spirit upon him, and he shall hear a message, and shall return to his own country, and I will cause him to fall by the sword in his own country.

text.NRSV: The Rabshakeh returned, and found the king of Assyria fighting against Libnah; for he had heard that the king had left Lachish.
text.Rotherham: So Rabshakeh returned, and found the king of Assyria, warring against Libnah,—for he had heard, that he had broken up from Lachish.
text.DRB: And Rabsaces returned, and found the king of the Assyrians besieging Lobna. For he had heard that he was departed from Lachis.

text.NRSV: Now the king heard concerning King Tirhakah of Ethiopia, “He has set out to fight against you.” When he heard it, he sent messengers to Hezekiah, saying,
text.Rotherham: And he heard it reported concerning Tirhakah king of Ethiopia, saying, He hath come forth to fight with thee,—so when he heard it, he sent messengers unto Hezekiah, saying:
text.DRB: And he heard say about Tharaca the king of Ethiopia: He is come forth to fight against thee. And when he heard it, he sent messengers to Ezechias, saying:

text.NRSV: “Thus shall you speak to King Hezekiah of Judah: Do not let your God on whom you rely deceive you by promising that Jerusalem will not be given into the hand of the king of Assyria.
text.Rotherham: Thus, shall ye surely speak unto Hezekiah king of Judah, saying, Let not thy God, in whom, thou, art trusting, beguile thee, saying,—Jerusalem, shall not be given over, into the hand of the king of Assyria.
text.DRB: Thus shall you speak to Ezechias the king of Juda, saying: Let not thy God deceive thee, in whom thou trustest, saying: Jerusalem shall not be given into the hands of the king of the Assyrians.

text.NRSV: See, you have heard what the kings of Assyria have done to all lands, destroying them utterly. And shall you be delivered?
text.Rotherham: Lo! thou, thyself, hast heard what the kings of Assyria have done to all the lands in devoting them to destruction,—and shalt, thou, be delivered?
text.DRB: Behold thou hast heard all that the kings of the Assyrians have done to all countries which they have destroyed, and canst thou be delivered?

text.NRSV: Have the gods of the nations delivered them, the nations that my predecessors destroyed, Gozan, Haran, Rezeph, and the people of Eden who were in Telassar?
text.Rotherham: Did the gods of the nations, deliver them, whom my fathers destroyed,—Gozan, and Haran,—and Rezeph, and the sons of Eden, who were in Telassar?
text.DRB: Have the gods of the nations delivered them whom my fathers have destroyed, Gozam, and Haram, and Reseph, and the children of Eden, that were in Thalassar?

text.NRSV: Where is the king of Hamath, the king of Arpad, the king of the city of Sepharvaim, the king of Hena, or the king of Ivvah?”
text.Rotherham: Where are the king of Hamath, and the king of Arpad, and the king of the city of Sepharvaim,—of Hena, and Ivvah?
text.DRB: Where is the king of Emath, and the king of Arphad, and the king of the city of Sepharvaim, of Ana, and of Ava?

text.NRSV: Hezekiah received the letter from the hand of the messengers and read it; then Hezekiah went up to the house of the Lord and spread it before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, when Hezekiah had received the letter at the hand of the messengers, and had read it, then went he up to the house of Yahweh, and Hezekiah spread it out, before Yahweh.
text.DRB: And Ezechias took the letter from the hand of the messengers, and read it, and went up to the house of the Lord, and Ezechias spread it before the Lord.

text.NRSV: And Hezekiah prayed to the Lord, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Hezekiah prayed unto Yahweh saying:
text.DRB: And Ezechias prayed to the Lord, saying:

text.NRSV: “O Lord of hosts, God of Israel, who are enthroned on the cherubim, you are God, you alone, of all the kingdoms of the earth; you have made heaven and earth.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh of hosts, God of Israel—inhabiting the cherubim, Thou thyself, art GOD, even thou alone, for all the kingdoms of the earth: Thou, didst make the heavens and the earth.
text.DRB: O Lord of hosts, God of Israel, who sittest upon the cherubims, thou alone art the God of all the kingdoms of the earth, thou hast made heaven and earth.

text.NRSV: Incline your ear, O Lord, and hear; open your eyes, O Lord, and see; hear all the words of Sennacherib, which he has sent to mock the living God.
text.Rotherham: Bow down, O Yahweh, thine ear—and hear, Open, O Yahweh, thine eyes—and see,—Yea hear thou all the words of Sennacherib, who hath sent—to reproach a Living God.
text.DRB: Incline, O Lord, thy ear, and hear: open, O Lord, thy eyes, and see, and hear all the words of Sennacherib, which he hath sent to blaspheme the living God.

text.NRSV: Truly, O Lord, the kings of Assyria have laid waste all the nations and their lands,
text.Rotherham: Of a truth, O Yahweh,—the kings of Assyria have devoted to destruction all the countries, and their land;
text.DRB: For of a truth, O Lord, the kings of the Assyrians have laid waste lands, and their countries.

text.NRSV: and have cast their gods into the fire, for they were no gods, but the work of human hands, wood and stone; therefore they were destroyed.
text.Rotherham: and have put their gods in the fire,—for, no-gods, were they, but the work of the hands of men wood and stone and so they destroyed them.
text.DRB: And they have cast their gods into the fire, for they were not gods, but the works of men's hands, of wood and stone: and they broke them in pieces.

text.NRSV: So now, O Lord our God, save us from his hand, so that all the kingdoms of the earth may know that you alone are the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, O Yahweh our God, save us, out of his hand,—That all the kingdoms of the earth, may know, That, thou, art Yahweh, thou alone.
text.DRB: And now, O Lord our God, save us out of his hand: and let all the kingdoms of the earth know, that thou only art the Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Isaiah son of Amoz sent to Hezekiah, saying: “Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel: Because you have prayed to me concerning King Sennacherib of Assyria,
text.Rotherham: Then Isaiah son of Amoz sent unto Hezekiah saying,—Thus, saith Yahweh, God of Israel, In that thou hast prayed unto me concerning Sennacherib king of Assyria,
text.DRB: And Isaias the son of Amos sent to Ezechias, saying: Thus saith the Lord the God of Israel: For the prayer thou hast made to me concerning Sennacherib the king of the Assyrians:

text.NRSV: this is the word that the Lord has spoken concerning him: She despises you, she scorns you— virgin daughter Zion; she tosses her head—behind your back, daughter Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: this, is the word which Yahweh hath spoken concerning him,—The virgin daughter of Zion, laugheth thee to scorn—mocketh thee, The daughter of Jerusalem after thee doth wag her head,
text.DRB: This is the word which the Lord hath spoken of him: The virgin the daughter of Sion hath despised thee, and laughed thee to scorn: the daughter of Jerusalem hath wagged the head after thee.

text.NRSV: “Whom have you reviled and blasphemed? Against whom have you raised your voice and haughtily lifted your eyes? Against the Holy One of Israel!
text.Rotherham: Whom, hast thou reproached and insulted? And, against whom, hast thou lifted high thy voice? Yea thou hast proudly raised thine eyes, against the Holy One of Israel.
text.DRB: Whom hast thou reproached, and whom hast thou blasphemed, and against whom hast thou exalted thy voice, and lifted up thy eyes on high? Against the Holy One of Israel.

text.NRSV: By your servants you have mocked the Lord, and you have said, ‘With my many chariots I have gone up the heights of the mountains, to the far recesses of Lebanon; I felled its tallest cedars, its choicest cypresses; I came to its remotest height, its most fruitful forest.
text.Rotherham: Through thy servants, thou hast reproached My Lord and hast said,—With my multitude of chariots, have I ascended The height of the mountains The recesses of Lebanon, That I may cut down Its tallest cedars Its choicest firs, That I may enter Its highest summit, Its thick garden forest:
text.DRB: By the hand of thy servants thou hast reproached the Lord: and hast said: With the multitude of my chariots I have gone up to the height of the mountains, to the top of Libanus: and I will cut down its tall cedars, and its choice fir trees, and will enter to the top of its height, to the forest of its Carmel .

text.NRSV: I dug wells and drank waters, I dried up with the sole of my foot all the streams of Egypt.’
text.Rotherham: I, have digged, and drunk waters,—That I may dry up, with the soles of my feet, all the Nile-streams of Egypt.
text.DRB: I have digged, and drunk water, and have dried up with the sole of my foot, all the rivers shut up in banks.

text.NRSV: “Have you not heard that I determined it long ago? I planned from days of old what now I bring to pass, that you should make fortified cities crash into heaps of ruins,
text.Rotherham: Hast thou not heard—That, long ago, that, is what I appointed, And from days of old, devised? Now, have I brought it to pass, That thou mightest Serve to lay waste, in desolate ruins, fortified cities;
text.DRB: Hast thou not heard what I have done to him of old? from the days of old I have formed it: and now I have brought it to effect: and it hath come to pass that hills fighting together, and fenced cities should be destroyed.

text.NRSV: while their inhabitants, shorn of strength, are dismayed and confounded; they have become like plants of the field and like tender grass, like grass on the housetops, blighted before it is grown.
text.Rotherham: And their inhabitants, being powerless, were overthrown and put to shame—They became—Grass of the field and Young herbage, Grass on housetops, and Seed parched before it came up.
text.DRB: The inhabitants of them were weak of hand, they trembled, and were confounded: they became like the grass of the field, and the herb of the pasture, and like the grass of the housetops, which withered before it was ripe.

text.NRSV: “I know your rising up and your sitting down, your going out and your coming in, and your raging against me.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, thine abode and thy coming out and thy going in, I know,—and thy raging against me.
text.DRB: I know thy dwelling, and thy going out, and thy coming in, and thy rage against me.

text.NRSV: Because you have raged against me and your arrogance has come to my ears, I will put my hook in your nose and my bit in your mouth; I will turn you back on the way by which you came.
text.Rotherham: Because, thy raging against me, and thy contempt, have come up into mine ears, Therefore will I put My ring in thy nose, and My bit in thy lips, And will turn thee back, by the way by which thou camest.
text.DRB: When thou wast mad against me, thy pride came up to my ears: therefore I will put a ring in thy nose, and a bit between thy lips, and I will turn thee back by the way by which thou camest.

text.NRSV: “And this shall be the sign for you: This year eat what grows of itself, and in the second year what springs from that; then in the third year sow, reap, plant vineyards, and eat their fruit.
text.Rotherham: And, this unto thee, is the sign, Eating this year, the growth of scattered seeds, And in the second year, that which shooteth up of itself,—Then in the third year, Sow ye—and reap, and Plant ye vineyards, and eat the fruit thereof.
text.DRB: But to thee this shall be a sign: Eat this year the things that spring of themselves, and in the second year eat fruits: but in the third year sow and reap, and giant vineyards, and eat the fruit of them.

text.NRSV: The surviving remnant of the house of Judah shall again take root downward, and bear fruit upward;
text.Rotherham: Then shall the escaped of the house of Judah that remain, again, Take root downward,—And bear fruit upward.
text.DRB: And that which shall be saved of the house of Juda, and which is left, shall take root downward, and shall bear fruit upward:

text.NRSV: for from Jerusalem a remnant shall go out, and from Mount Zion a band of survivors. The zeal of the Lord of hosts will do this.
text.Rotherham: For, out of Jerusalem, shall come forth a remnant, And that which hath escaped, out of Mount Zion,—the jealousy of Yahweh of hosts, will perform, this.
text.DRB: For out of Jerusalem shall go forth a, remnant, and salvation from mount Sion: the zeal of the Lord of hosts shall do this.

text.NRSV: “Therefore thus says the Lord concerning the king of Assyria: He shall not come into this city, shoot an arrow there, come before it with a shield, or cast up a siegework against it.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, thus, saith Yahweh, concerning the king of Assyria, He shall not enter this city, Nor shoot there, an arrow,—Nor attack it with shield, Nor cast up against it a mound:
text.DRB: Wherefore thus saith the Lord concerning the king of the Assyrians: He shall not come into this city, nor shoot an arrow into it, nor come before it with shield, nor cast a trench about it.

text.NRSV: By the way that he came, by the same he shall return; he shall not come into this city, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: By the way that he Came in, By the Same, shall he return,—And into this city, shall he not enter, Declareth Yahweh.
text.DRB: By the way that he came, he shall return, and into this city he shall not come, saith the Lord.

text.NRSV: For I will defend this city to save it, for my own sake and for the sake of my servant David.”
text.Rotherham: Thus will I throw a covering over this city to save it,—For mine own sake, And for the sake of David my servant.
text.DRB: And I will protect this city, and will save it for my own sake, and for the sake of David my servant.

text.NRSV: Then the angel of the Lord set out and struck down one hundred eighty-five thousand in the camp of the Assyrians; when people got up early in the morning, there were all the dead bodies.
text.Rotherham: Then went forth the messenger of Yahweh, and smote—in the camp of the Assyrians—a hundred and eighty-five thousand,—and when men arose early in the morning, lo! they were all, dead bodies!
text.DRB: And the angel of the Lord went out, and slew in the camp of the Assyrians a hundred and eighty-five thousand. And they arose in the morning, and behold they were all dead corpses.

text.NRSV: Then King Sennacherib of Assyria left, went home, and lived at Nineveh.
text.Rotherham: So Sennacherib the king of Assyria brake up, and went his way, and returned,—and remained in Nineveh.
text.DRB: And Sennacherib the king of the Assyrians went out and departed, and returned, and dwelt in Ninive.

text.NRSV: As he was worshiping in the house of his god Nisroch, his sons Adrammelech and Sharezer killed him with the sword, and they escaped into the land of Ararat. His son Esar-haddon succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as he was bowing down in the house of Nisroch his god, that, Adrammelech and Sharezer his sons, smote him with the sword, howbeit, they, escaped into the land of Ararat,—and, Esarhaddon his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.DRB: And it came to pass, as he was worshipping in the temple of Nesroch his god, that Adramelech and Sarasar his sons slew him with the sword: and they fled into the land of Ararat, and Asarhaddon his son reigned in his stead.

text.NRSV: In those days Hezekiah became sick and was at the point of death. The prophet Isaiah son of Amoz came to him, and said to him, “Thus says the Lord: Set your house in order, for you shall die; you shall not recover.”
text.Rotherham: In those days, was Hezekiah sick, unto death,—and Isaiah the prophet son of Amoz came in unto him, and said unto him—Thus, saith Yahweh, Set in order thy house, for, about to die thou art and shalt not recover.
text.DRB: In those days Ezechias was sick even to death, and Isaias the son of Amos the prophet came unto him, and said to him: Thus saith the Lord: Take order with thy house, for thou shalt die, and not live.

text.NRSV: Then Hezekiah turned his face to the wall, and prayed to the Lord:
text.Rotherham: Then Hezekiah turned his face unto the wall,—and prayed unto Yahweh;
text.DRB: And Ezechias turned his face toward the wall, and prayed to the Lord,

text.NRSV: “Remember now, O Lord, I implore you, how I have walked before you in faithfulness with a whole heart, and have done what is good in your sight.” And Hezekiah wept bitterly.
text.Rotherham: and said,—I beseech thee, O Yahweh, remember, I pray thee, how I have walked before thee in faithfulness and with an undivided heart, and, that which is good in thine eyes, have I done. And Hezekiah wept aloud.
text.DRB: And said: I beseech thee, O Lord, remember how I have walked before thee in truth, and with a perfect heart, and have done that which is good in thy sight. And Ezechias wept with great weeping.

text.NRSV: Then the word of the Lord came to Isaiah:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto, Isaiah, saying:
text.DRB: And the word of the Lord came to Isaias, saying:

text.NRSV: “Go and say to Hezekiah, Thus says the Lord, the God of your ancestor David: I have heard your prayer, I have seen your tears; I will add fifteen years to your life.
text.Rotherham: Go, and say unto Hezekiah—Thus, saith Yahweh, the God of David thy father: I have heard thy prayer, I have seen thy tears,—Behold me! about to add unto thy days, fifteen years;
text.DRB: Go and say to Ezechias: Thus saith the Lord the God of David thy father: I have heard thy prayer, and I have seen thy tears: behold I will add to thy days fifteen years:

text.NRSV: I will deliver you and this city out of the hand of the king of Assyria, and defend this city.
text.Rotherham: And out of the hand of the king of Assyria, will I deliver thee, and this city; And I will throw a covering over this city.
text.DRB: And I will deliver thee and this city out of the hand of the king of the Assyrians, and I will protect it.

text.NRSV: “This is the sign to you from the Lord, that the Lord will do this thing that he has promised:
text.Rotherham: And, this, to thee, shall be the sign from Yahweh,—that Yahweh will do this thing which he hath spoken:—
text.DRB: And this shall be a sign to thee from the Lord, that the Lord will do this word which he hath spoken:

text.NRSV: See, I will make the shadow cast by the declining sun on the dial of Ahaz turn back ten steps.” So the sun turned back on the dial the ten steps by which it had declined.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! causing the shadow on the steps, which hath come gone down on the steps of Ahaz with the sun, to return, backwards ten steps. So the sun returned ten steps, by the steps which it had come down.
text.DRB: Behold I will bring again the shadow of the lines, by which it is now gone down in the sun dial of Achaz with the sun, ten lines backward. And the sun returned ten lines by the degrees by which it was gone down.

text.NRSV: A writing of King Hezekiah of Judah, after he had been sick and had recovered from his sickness:
text.Rotherham: the writing of Hezekiah king of Judah, when he had been sick and then recovered from his sickness:
text.DRB: The writing of Ezechias king of Juda, when he had been sick, and was recovered of his sickness.

text.NRSV: I said: In the noontide of my days I must depart; I am consigned to the gates of Sheol for the rest of my years.
text.Rotherham: I, said—In the noontide of my days, I must enter the gates of hades,—I am deprived of the residue of my years!
text.DRB: I said: In the midst of my days I shall go to the gates of hell : I sought for the residue of my years.

text.NRSV: I said, I shall not see the Lord in the land of the living; I shall look upon mortals no more among the inhabitants of the world.
text.Rotherham: I said—I shall not see Yah, Yah, in the land of the living, I shall discern the son of earth no longer, with the dwellers in the quiet land.
text.DRB: I said: I shall not see the Lord God in the land of the living. I shall behold man no more, nor the inhabitant of rest.

text.NRSV: My dwelling is plucked up and removed from me like a shepherd’s tent; like a weaver I have rolled up my life; he cuts me off from the loom; from day to night you bring me to an end;
text.Rotherham: My dwelling, hath been broken up. And is stripped from me, like a shepherd’s tent,—I have rolled up—as a weaver—my life From the loom, doth he cut me off, From day until night, [I said] —Thou wilt finish me.
text.DRB: My generation is at an end, and it is rolled away from me, as a shepherd's tent. My life is cut off, as by a weaver: whilst I was yet but beginning, he cut me off: from morning even to night thou wilt make an end of me.

text.NRSV: I cry for help until morning; like a lion he breaks all my bones; from day to night you bring me to an end.
text.Rotherham: I cried out, until morning, like a lion, Thus, will he break all my bones! From day until night, Thou wilt finish me!
text.DRB: I hoped till morning, as a lion so hath he broken all my bones: from morning even to night thou wilt make an end of me.

text.NRSV: Like a swallow or a crane I clamor, I moan like a dove. My eyes are weary with looking upward. O Lord, I am oppressed; be my security!
text.Rotherham: As a twittering swallow, so, do I chatter, I coo as a dove,—Mine eyes languish through looking on high, O My Lord! distress is upon me—my Surety!
text.DRB: I will cry like a young swallow, I will meditate like a dove: my eyes are weakened looking upward: Lord, I suffer violence, answer thou for me.

text.NRSV: But what can I say? For he has spoken to me, and he himself has done it. All my sleep has fled because of the bitterness of my soul.
text.Rotherham: What can I say? Since he hath promised for me, Himself, will perform. I will go softly, all my years. Because of the bitterness of my soul,
text.DRB: What shall I say, or what shall he answer for me, whereas he himself hath done it? I will recount to thee all my years in the bitterness of my soul.

text.NRSV: O Lord, by these things people live, and in all these is the life of my spirit. Oh, restore me to health and make me live!
text.Rotherham: O My Lord! on those things do men live,—And, altogether in them, is the life of my spirit, When thou hast strengthened me and made me live.
text.DRB: O Lord, if man's life be such, and the life of my spirit be in such things as these, thou shalt correct me, and make me to live.

text.NRSV: Surely it was for my welfare that I had great bitterness; but you have held back my life from the pit of destruction, for you have cast all my sins behind your back.
text.Rotherham: Lo! for well-being, I had bitterness—bitterness,—But, thou, cleaving unto my soul, hast raised me from the pit of corruption, For thou hast cast, behind thy back all my sins.
text.DRB: Behold in peace is my bitterness most bitter: but thou hast delivered my soul that it should not perish, thou hast cast all my sins behind thy back.

text.NRSV: For Sheol cannot thank you, death cannot praise you; those who go down to the Pit cannot hope for your faithfulness.
text.Rotherham: For, hades, cannot praise thee Nor, death, celebrate thee,—They who go down to the pit cannot wait for thy faithfulness.
text.DRB: For hell shall not confess to thee, neither shall death praise thee: nor shall they that go down into the pit, look for thy truth.

text.NRSV: The living, the living, they thank you, as I do this day; fathers make known to children your faithfulness.
text.Rotherham: The living, the living, he, can praise thee, As I do this day,—A father, to his children, can make known thy faithfulness.
text.DRB: The living, the living, he shall give praise to thee, as I do this day: the father shall make thy truth known to the children.

text.NRSV: The Lord will save me, and we will sing to stringed instruments all the days of our lives, at the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, [was willing] to save me,—Therefore, on my stringed instruments, will we play—All the days of our life By the house of Yahweh.
text.DRB: O Lord, save me, and we will sing our psalms all the days of our life in the house of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Now Isaiah had said, “Let them take a lump of figs, and apply it to the boil, so that he may recover.”
text.Rotherham: And Isaiah had said, Let them take a cake of figs, and let them press it over the boil, that he may recover.
text.DRB: Now Isaias had ordered that they should take a lump of figs, and lay it as it plaster upon the wound, and that he should be healed.

text.NRSV: Hezekiah also had said, “What is the sign that I shall go up to the house of the Lord?”
text.Rotherham: And Hezekiah had said—What is the sign—that I shall go up unto the house of Yahweh?
text.DRB: And Ezechias had said: What shall be the sign that I shall go up to the house of the Lord?

text.NRSV: At that time Merodach-baladan son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent envoys with letters and a present to Hezekiah, for he heard that he had been sick and had recovered.
text.Rotherham: At that time, Merodach-baladan son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent letters and a present unto Hezekiah,—for he had heard that Hezekiah had been sick, and had recovered.
text.DRB: At that time Merodach Baladan, the son of Baladan king of Babylon, sent letters and presents to Ezechias: for he had heard that he had been sick and was recovered.

text.NRSV: Hezekiah welcomed them; he showed them his treasure house, the silver, the gold, the spices, the precious oil, his whole armory, all that was found in his storehouses. There was nothing in his house or in all his realm that Hezekiah did not show them.
text.Rotherham: And Hezekiah, rejoiced over them, and showed them his house of precious things—the silver and the gold and the spices and the precious ointment and all his armoury—and all that was found among his treasures,—there was nothing, which Hezekiah, did not show them—in his house or in all his dominion.
text.DRB: And Ezechias rejoiced at their coming, and he shewed them the storehouses of his aromatical spices, and of the silver, and of the gold, and of the sweet odours, and of the precious ointment, and all the storehouses of his furniture, and all things that were found in his treasures. There was nothing in his house, nor in all his dominion that Ezechias shewed them not.

text.NRSV: Then the prophet Isaiah came to King Hezekiah and said to him, “What did these men say? From where did they come to you?” Hezekiah answered, “They have come to me from a far country, from Babylon.”
text.Rotherham: Then came Isaiah the prophet, unto King Hezekiah,—and said unto him—What said these men? and whence came they unto thee? And Hezekiah said, From a land far away, came they unto me, from Babylon!
text.DRB: Then Isaias the prophet came to king Ezechias, and said to him: What said these men, and from whence came they to thee? And Ezechias said: From a far country they came to me, from Babylon.

text.NRSV: He said, “What have they seen in your house?” Hezekiah answered, “They have seen all that is in my house; there is nothing in my storehouses that I did not show them.”
text.Rotherham: And he said, What have they seen in thy house? Then said Hezekiah,—All that is in my house, have they seen, there is nothing which I shewed them not among my treasures.
text.DRB: And he said: What saw they in thy house? And Ezechias said: All things that are in my house have they seen, there was not any thing which I have not shewn them in my treasures.

text.NRSV: Then Isaiah said to Hezekiah, “Hear the word of the Lord of hosts:
text.Rotherham: Then said Isaiah unto Hezekiah,—Hear thou the word of Yahweh of hosts:
text.DRB: And Isaias said to Ezechias: Hear the word of the Lord of hosts.

text.NRSV: Days are coming when all that is in your house, and that which your ancestors have stored up until this day, shall be carried to Babylon; nothing shall be left, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Lo! days are coming, when all that is in thy house, and that which thy fathers have treasured up until this day, shall be carried away unto Babylon,—nothing, shall be left, Saith Yahweh.
text.DRB: Behold the days shall come, that all that is in thy house, and that thy fathers have laid up in store until this day, shall be carried away into Babylon: there shall not any thing be left, saith the Lord.

text.NRSV: Some of your own sons who are born to you shall be taken away; they shall be eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.”
text.Rotherham: And of thy sons who shall issue from thee whom thou shalt beget, shall they take away,—and they shall become eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.
text.DRB: And of thy children, that shall issue from thee, whom thou shalt beget, they shall take away, and they shall be eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.

text.NRSV: Then Hezekiah said to Isaiah, “The word of the Lord that you have spoken is good.” For he thought, “There will be peace and security in my days.”
text.Rotherham: And Hezekiah said unto Isaiah, Good is the word of Yahweh, which thou hast spoken. And he said, Surely, there shall be peace and stability in my days.
text.DRB: And Ezechias said to Isaias: The word of the Lord, which he hath spoken, is good. And he said: Only let peace and truth be in my days.

text.NRSV: Comfort, O comfort my people, says your God.
text.Rotherham: Comfort ye—comfort ye my people,—Saith your God.
text.DRB: Be comforted, be comforted, my people, saith your God.

text.NRSV: Speak tenderly to Jerusalem, and cry to her that she has served her term, that her penalty is paid, that she has received from the Lord’s hand double for all her sins.
text.Rotherham: Speak ye unto the heart of Jerusalem, And cry unto her,—That accomplished is her warfare, That accepted is her punishment,—That she hath received, at the hand of Yahweh, According to the full measure of all her sins.
text.DRB: Speak ye to the heart of Jerusalem, and call to her: for her evil is come to an end, her iniquity is forgiven: she hath received of the hand of the Lord double for all her sins.

text.NRSV: A voice cries out: “In the wilderness prepare the way of the Lord, make straight in the desert a highway for our God.
text.Rotherham: A voice of one crying!—In the desert, prepare ye the way of Yahweh,—Make smooth in the waste plain a highway for our God:
text.DRB: The voice of one crying in the desert: Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make straight in the wilderness the paths of our God.

text.NRSV: Every valley shall be lifted up, and every mountain and hill be made low; the uneven ground shall become level, and the rough places a plain.
text.Rotherham: Let, every valley, be exalted, And every mountain and hill, be made low,—And, the steep ground become, level, And, the chain of hills—a plain:
text.DRB: Every valley shall be exalted, and every mountain and hill shall be made low, and the crooked shall become straight, and the rough ways plain.

text.NRSV: Then the glory of the Lord shall be revealed, and all people shall see it together, for the mouth of the Lord has spoken.”
text.Rotherham: Then shall be revealed the glory of Yahweh,—And all flesh shall see it together, For, the mouth of Yahweh, hath spoken!
text.DRB: And the glory of the Lord shall be revealed, and all flesh together shall see, that the mouth of the Lord hath spoken.

text.NRSV: A voice says, “Cry out!” And I said, “What shall I cry?” All people are grass, their constancy is like the flower of the field.
text.Rotherham: A voice saying Cry! And one said—What should I cry? All flesh, is grass, And, all the grace thereof, like the flower of the field:
text.DRB: The voice of one, saying: Cry. And I said: What shall I cry? All flesh is grass, and all the glory thereof as the flower of the field.

text.NRSV: The grass withers, the flower fades, when the breath of the Lord blows upon it; surely the people are grass.
text.Rotherham: The grass hath withered The flower hath faded, Because, the breath of Yahweh, hath blown upon it! Surely the people is grass!
text.DRB: The grass is withered, and the flower is fallen, because the spirit of the Lord hath blown upon it. Indeed the people is grass:

text.NRSV: The grass withers, the flower fades; but the word of our God will stand forever.
text.Rotherham: The grass, hath withered, The flower, hath faded,—But the word of our God, shall stand unto times age-abiding!
text.DRB: The grass is withered, and the flower is fallen: but the word of our Lord endureth for ever.

text.NRSV: Get you up to a high mountain, O Zion, herald of good tidings; lift up your voice with strength, O Jerusalem, herald of good tidings, lift it up, do not fear; say to the cities of Judah, “Here is your God!”
text.Rotherham: To a high mountain, get ye up, O herald—band of Zion, Lift high with strength your voice, O herald—band of Jerusalem,—Lift it high, do not fear, Say to the cities of Judah—Lo! your God!
text.DRB: Get thee up upon a high mountain, thou that bringest good tidings to Sion: lift up thy voice with strength, thou that bringest good tidings to Jerusalem: lift it up, fear not. Say to the cities of Juda: Behold your God:

text.NRSV: See, the Lord God comes with might, and his arm rules for him; his reward is with him, and his recompense before him.
text.Rotherham: Lo! My Lord, Yahweh, as a mighty one, doth come, And, his own arm, is about to rule for him,—Lo! his reward, is with him, And, his recompense, before him;
text.DRB: Behold the Lord God shall come with strength, and his arm shall rule: Behold his reward is with him and his work is before him.

text.NRSV: He will feed his flock like a shepherd; he will gather the lambs in his arms, and carry them in his bosom, and gently lead the mother sheep.
text.Rotherham: Like a shepherd, his flock, will he tend, In his own arm, will he take up the lambs, And in his own bosom, will he carry [them],—Them which are with young, will he lead to a place of rest.
text.DRB: He shall feed his flock like a shepherd: he shall gather together the lambs with his arm, and shall take them up in his bosom, and he himself shall carry them that are with young.

text.NRSV: Who has measured the waters in the hollow of his hand and marked off the heavens with a span, enclosed the dust of the earth in a measure, and weighed the mountains in scales and the hills in a balance?
text.Rotherham: Who hath measured, with the hollow of his hand, the waters. Or the heavens with a span, hath meted out, Or hath comprehended, in a measure, the dust of the earth, Or weighed, in scales, the mountains, Or the hills, in a balance?
text.DRB: Who hath measured the waters in the hollow of his hand, and weighed the heavens with his palm? who hath poised with three fingers the bulk of the earth, and weighed the mountains in scales, and the hills in a balance?

text.NRSV: Who has directed the spirit of the Lord, or as his counselor has instructed him?
text.Rotherham: Who hath proved the spirit of Yahweh? Or being his counsellor hath been giving him knowledge?
text.DRB: Who hath forwarded the spirit of the Lord? or who hath been his counsellor, and hath taught him?

text.NRSV: Whom did he consult for his enlightenment, and who taught him the path of justice? Who taught him knowledge, and showed him the way of understanding?
text.Rotherham: With whom hath he taken counsel—And he hath given him intelligence, And instructed him in the path of justice,—And taught him knowledge, And in the way of intelligence, hath been giving him understanding?
text.DRB: With whom hath he consulted, and who hath instructed him, and taught him the path of justice, and taught him knowledge, and shewed him the way of understanding?

text.NRSV: Even the nations are like a drop from a bucket, and are accounted as dust on the scales; see, he takes up the islands like fine dust.
text.Rotherham: Lo! nations, Are us a drop on a bucket, And as fine dust on a balance, are accounted,—Lo! islands, like an atom, can he hoist;
text.DRB: Behold the Gentiles are as a drop of a bucket, and are counted as the smallest grain of a balance: behold the islands are as a little dust.

text.NRSV: Lebanon would not provide fuel enough, nor are its animals enough for a burnt offering.
text.Rotherham: And, Lebanon, is not sufficient to burn,—Nor, the beasts thereof sufficient for an ascending-sacrifice!
text.DRB: And Libanus shall not be enough to burn, nor the beasts thereof sufficient for a burnt offering.

text.NRSV: All the nations are as nothing before him; they are accounted by him as less than nothing and emptiness.
text.Rotherham: All nations, are as nothing before him,—A thing of nought or a waste, are they accounted unto him?
text.DRB: All nations are before him as if they had no being at all, and are counted to him as nothing, and vanity.

text.NRSV: To whom then will you liken God, or what likeness compare with him?
text.Rotherham: Unto whom then can ye liken GOD? Or, what likeness, can ye compare unto him?
text.DRB: To whom then have you likened God? or what image will you make for him?

text.NRSV: An idol? —A workman casts it, and a goldsmith overlays it with gold, and casts for it silver chains.
text.Rotherham: The image, hath been cast by an artificer, And a goldsmith, with gold, overlayeth it,—And, chains of silver, he worketh.
text.DRB: Hath the workman cast a graven statue? or hath the goldsmith formed it with gold, or the silversmith with plates of silver?

text.NRSV: As a gift one chooses mulberry wood—wood that will not rot—then seeks out a skilled artisan to set up an image that will not totter.
text.Rotherham: The needy offerer, of a tree that will not rot, maketh choice,—A skilled artificer, seeketh he out for himself to construct an image that shall not totter,
text.DRB: He hath chosen strong wood, and that will not rot: the skillful workman seeketh how he may set up an idol that may not be moved.

text.NRSV: Have you not known? Have you not heard? Has it not been told you from the beginning? Have you not understood from the foundations of the earth?
text.Rotherham: Have ye never taken note? Have ye never heard? Hath it not from the beginning, been told you? Have ye not been led to discern, from the foundations of the earth?
text.DRB: Do you not know? hath it not been heard? hath it not been told you from the beginning? have you not understood the foundations of the earth?

text.NRSV: It is he who sits above the circle of the earth, and its inhabitants are like grasshoppers; who stretches out the heavens like a curtain, and spreads them like a tent to live in;
text.Rotherham: It is he who sitteth upon the circle of the earth, While the inhabitants thereof are, as grass-hoppers,—Who stretcheth forth, as a curtain, the heavens, And spreadeth them out as a tent to dwell in;
text.DRB: It is he that sitteth upon the globe of the earth, and the inhabitants thereof are as locusts: he that stretcheth out the heavens as nothing, and spreadeth them out as a tent to dwell in.

text.NRSV: who brings princes to naught, and makes the rulers of the earth as nothing.
text.Rotherham: Who delivereth dignitaries to nothingness,—Judges of earth, like a desolation, hath he made:
text.DRB: He that bringeth the searchers of secrets to nothing, that hath made the judges of the earth as vanity.

text.NRSV: Scarcely are they planted, scarcely sown, scarcely has their stem taken root in the earth, when he blows upon them, and they wither, and the tempest carries them off like stubble.
text.Rotherham: Scarcely have they been planted, Scarcely have they been sown, Scarcely hath their stock, begun to take root in the earth, When he hath just blown upon them and they have withered, And, a whirlwind, as though they had been chaff, carrieth them away.
text.DRB: And surely their stock was neither planted, nor sown, nor rooted in the earth: suddenly he hath blown upon them, and they are withered, and a whirlwind shall take them away as stubble.

text.NRSV: To whom then will you compare me, or who is my equal? says the Holy One.
text.Rotherham: Unto whom then, can ye liken me, or can I be equal? Saith the Holy One.
text.DRB: And to whom have ye likened me, or made me equal, saith the Holy One?

text.NRSV: Lift up your eyes on high and see: Who created these? He who brings out their host and numbers them, calling them all by name; because he is great in strength, mighty in power, not one is missing.
text.Rotherham: Lift on high your eyes—and see who hath created these, That bringeth forth, by number, their host,—To all of them by name, doth call, Because of the abundance of vigour and alertness of strength, not one, is missing!
text.DRB: Lift up your eyes on high, and see who hath created these things: who bringeth out their host by number, and calleth them all by their names: by the greatness of his might, and strength, and power, not one of them was missing.

text.NRSV: Why do you say, O Jacob, and speak, O Israel, “My way is hidden from the Lord, and my right is disregarded by my God”?
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, shouldest thou say, O Jacob, or speak, O Israel,—Hidden is my path from Yahweh, And from my God, my vindication, will pass?
text.DRB: Why sayest thou, O Jacob, and speakest, O Israel: My way is hid from the Lord, and my judgment is passed over from my God?

text.NRSV: Have you not known? Have you not heard? The Lord is the everlasting God, the Creator of the ends of the earth. He does not faint or grow weary; his understanding is unsearchable.
text.Rotherham: Hast thou not known, Hast thou not heard, That The God of age-past time—Yahweh The Creator of the ends of the earth Fainteth not neither groweth weary—There is no searching of his understanding:
text.DRB: Knowest thou not, or hast thou not heard? the Lord is the everlasting God, who hath created the ends of the earth: he shall not faint, nor labour, neither is there any searching out of his wisdom.

text.NRSV: He gives power to the faint, and strengthens the powerless.
text.Rotherham: Giving to him that fainteth, strength, And to him that hath no vigour, he causeth, power to abound?
text.DRB: It is he that giveth strength to the weary, and increaseth force and might to them that are not.

text.NRSV: Even youths will faint and be weary, and the young will fall exhausted;
text.Rotherham: Youths both faint and grow weary, And, young warriors—they fall, they fall;
text.DRB: Youths shall faint, and labour, and young men shall fall by infirmity.

text.NRSV: but those who wait for the Lord shall renew their strength, they shall mount up with wings like eagles, they shall run and not be weary, they shall walk and not faint.
text.Rotherham: But, they who wait for Yahweh, shall renew their strength, They shall mount on strong pinion like eagles,—They shall run and not grow weary, They shall walk and not faint.
text.DRB: But they that hope in the Lord shall renew their strength, they shall take wings as eagles, they shall run and not be weary, they shall walk and not faint.

text.NRSV: Listen to me in silence, O coastlands; let the peoples renew their strength; let them approach, then let them speak; let us draw near together for judgment.
text.Rotherham: Be silent [and hearken] unto me, O ye Coastlands, And let the Races of Men renew their strength,—Let them approach, then, let them speak, Together, for controversy, let us draw near:—
text.DRB: Let the islands keep silence before me, and the nations take new strength: let them come near, and then speak, let us come near to judgment together.

text.NRSV: Who has roused a victor from the east, summoned him to his service? He delivers up nations to him, and tramples kings under foot; he makes them like dust with his sword, like driven stubble with his bow.
text.Rotherham: Who roused up one from the East, In righteousness, called him to his feet,—Set before him nations And over kings, caused him to rule, His sword, made [them] like dust, His bow like driven chaff:
text.DRB: Who hath raised up the just one from the east, hath called him to follow him? he shall give the nations in his sight, and he shall rule over kings: he shall give them as the dust to his sword, as stubble driven by the wind, to his bow.

text.NRSV: He pursues them and passes on safely, scarcely touching the path with his feet.
text.Rotherham: He pursued them passed along safely,—Upon the path of his own feet, entered he not?
text.DRB: He shall pursue them, he shall pass in peace, no path shall appear after his feet.

text.NRSV: Who has performed and done this, calling the generations from the beginning? I, the Lord, am first, and will be with the last.
text.Rotherham: Who hath wrought and performed, Calling the generations, in advance? I, Yahweh, [who am] First, And with them who are last, I, am the Same!
text.DRB: Who hath wrought and done these things, calling the generations from the beginning? I the Lord, I am the first and the last.

text.NRSV: The coastlands have seen and are afraid, the ends of the earth tremble; they have drawn near and are come.
text.Rotherham: Coastlands have seen, so they fear, The ends of the earth, are in dread,—They have drawn near, and have come:
text.DRB: The islands saw it, and feared, the ends of the earth were astonished, they drew near, and came.

text.NRSV: Each one helps the other, saying to one’s neighbor, “Take courage!”
text.Rotherham: Every man, to his neighbour, giveth help,—And to his brother, saith Take courage!
text.DRB: Every one shall help his neighbour, and shall say to his brother: Be of good courage.

text.NRSV: The artisan encourages the goldsmith, and the one who smooths with the hammer encourages the one who strikes the anvil, saying of the soldering, “It is good”; and they fasten it with nails so that it cannot be moved.
text.Rotherham: So, the carver, hath encouraged, the goldsmith, he that maketh smooth with the hammer, him that smiteth the anvil,—Saying of the welding, It is, good, Then hath he fastened it with nails—it must not totter!
text.DRB: The coppersmith striking with the hammer encouraged him that forged at that time, saying: It is ready for soldering: and he strengthened it with nails, that it should not be moved.

text.NRSV: But you, Israel, my servant, Jacob, whom I have chosen, the offspring of Abraham, my friend;
text.Rotherham: But, thou, Israel, my Servant, Jacob whom I have chosen,—The seed of Abraham my loving one;
text.DRB: But thou Israel, art my servant, Jacob whom I have chosen, the seed of Abraham my friend:

text.NRSV: you whom I took from the ends of the earth, and called from its farthest corners, saying to you, “You are my servant, I have chosen you and not cast you off”;
text.Rotherham: Thou whom I have taken hold of from the ends of the earth, And from the extremities thereof, have called thee,—And said to thee, My Servant, thou! I have chosen thee and not cast thee off,
text.DRB: In whom I have taken thee from the ends of the earth, and from the remote parts thereof have called thee, and said to thee: Thou art my servant, I have chosen thee, and have not cast thee away.

text.NRSV: do not fear, for I am with you, do not be afraid, for I am your God; I will strengthen you, I will help you, I will uphold you with my victorious right hand.
text.Rotherham: Do not fear, for with thee, I am! Look not around, for, I, am thy God,—I have emboldened thee, Yea I have helped thee, Yea I have upheld thee with my righteous right-hand.
text.DRB: Fear not, for I am with thee: turn not aside, for I am thy God: I have strengthened thee, and have helped thee, and the right hand of my just one hath upheld thee.

text.NRSV: Yes, all who are incensed against you shall be ashamed and disgraced; those who strive against you shall be as nothing and shall perish.
text.Rotherham: Lo! they shall turn pale and be ashamed—All they who have been incensed against thee,—They shall become as nothing and perish—The men who have been thine accusers:
text.DRB: Behold all that fight against thee shall be confounded and ashamed, they shall be as nothing, and the men shall perish that strive against thee.

text.NRSV: You shall seek those who contend with you, but you shall not find them; those who war against you shall be as nothing at all.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt seek them, but shalt not find them—The men who have contended with thee, They shall become as nothing, and as a thing of nought—The men who have warred against thee;
text.DRB: Thou shalt seek them, and shalt not find the men that resist thee: they shall be as nothing: and as a thing consumed the men that war against thee.

text.NRSV: For I, the Lord your God, hold your right hand; it is I who say to you, “Do not fear, I will help you.”
text.Rotherham: For I, Yahweh thy God am firmly grasping thy right-hand,—Who am saying unto thee Do not fear! I, have become thy helper!
text.DRB: For I am the Lord thy God, who take thee by the hand, and say to thee: Fear not, I have helped thee.

text.NRSV: Do not fear, you worm Jacob, you insect Israel! I will help you, says the Lord; your Redeemer is the Holy One of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Do not fear, Thou worm Jacob, Ye men of Israel,—I, have become thy helper, Declareth Yahweh, And thy redeemer The Holy One of Israel.
text.DRB: Fear not, thou worm of Jacob, you that are dead of Israel: I have helped thee, saith the Lord: and thy Redeemer the Holy One of Israel.

text.NRSV: Now, I will make of you a threshing sledge, sharp, new, and having teeth; you shall thresh the mountains and crush them, and you shall make the hills like chaff.
text.Rotherham: Lo! I have made of thee a new pointed threshing sledge, owning teeth,—Thou shalt thresh mountains and crush them, And, hills—like chaff, shalt thou make:
text.DRB: I have made thee as a new thrashing wain, with teeth like a saw: thou shall thrash the mountains, and break them in pieces: and shalt make the hills as chaff.

text.NRSV: You shall winnow them and the wind shall carry them away, and the tempest shall scatter them. Then you shall rejoice in the Lord; in the Holy One of Israel you shall glory.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt fan them and, a wind, shall carry them away, And, a whirlwind, scatter, them,—But, thou, shalt exult in Yahweh, And, in the Holy One of Israel, shalt thou boast thyself.
text.DRB: Thou shalt fan them, and the wind shall carry them away, and the whirlwind shall scatter them: and thou shalt rejoice in the Lord, in the Holy One of Israel thou shalt be joyful.

text.NRSV: When the poor and needy seek water, and there is none, and their tongue is parched with thirst, I the Lord will answer them, I the God of Israel will not forsake them.
text.Rotherham: As for the oppressed and the needy. Seeking water when there is none, Their tongue with thirst being parched I—Yahweh, will answer them, The God of Israel, I will not forsake them:
text.DRB: The needy and the poor seek for waters, and there are none: their tongue hath been dry with thirst. I the Lord will hear them, I the God of Israel will not forsake them.

text.NRSV: I will make rivers gush forth on bare heights, and springs in the midst of valleys; I will make the wilderness a pool of water, and the dry land springs of water.
text.Rotherham: I will open, On bare hills rivers, and In the midst of plains, fountains,—I will make The desert, a lake of water, and Parched land, springs of water;
text.DRB: I will open rivers in the high hills, and fountains in the midst of the plains: I will turn the desert into pools of waters, and the impassable land into streams of waters.

text.NRSV: I will plant in the wilderness the cedar, the acacia, the myrtle, and the olive; I will set in the desert the cypress, the plane and the pine together,
text.Rotherham: I will set in the desert! Cedar, acacia, and myrtle, and oil-tree,—I will place, in the waste plain, Cypress, holm-oak, and sherbin-cedar, together
text.DRB: I will plant in the wilderness the cedar, and the thorn , and the myrtle, and the olive tree: I will set in the desert the fir tree, the elm, and the box tree together:

text.NRSV: so that all may see and know, all may consider and understand, that the hand of the Lord has done this, the Holy One of Israel has created it.
text.Rotherham: That men may see and observe and consider and understand at once, That, the hand of Yahweh, hath done this, That the Holy One of Israel hath created it.
text.DRB: That they may see and know, and consider, and understand together that the hand of the Lord hath done this, and the Holy One of Israel hath created it.

text.NRSV: Set forth your case, says the Lord; bring your proofs, says the King of Jacob.
text.Rotherham: Bring near your contention, Saith Yahweh,—Advance your defences, Saith the King of Jacob:
text.DRB: Bring your cause near, saith the Lord: bring hither, if you have any thing to allege, saith the King of Jacob.

text.NRSV: Let them bring them, and tell us what is to happen. Tell us the former things, what they are, so that we may consider them and know their outcome; or declare to us the things to come.
text.Rotherham: Let them advance them, and tell us, What shall happen,—Things known in advance—what they were, tell ye, That we may lay them to our heart and mark the after-story of them, Or, things yet to come, let us hear:
text.DRB: Let them come, and tell us all things that are to come: tell us the former things what they were: and we will set our heart upon them, and shall know the latter end of them, and tell us the things that are to come.

text.NRSV: Tell us what is to come hereafter, that we may know that you are gods; do good, or do harm, that we may be afraid and terrified.
text.Rotherham: Tell ye the events which shall be here-after, That we may perceive that gods, ye are,—Surely ye must do something—good or bad, That we may be amazed and behold it, at once.
text.DRB: Shew the things that are to come hereafter, and we shall know that ye are gods. Do ye also good or evil, if you can: and let us speak, and see together.

text.NRSV: You, indeed, are nothing and your work is nothing at all; whoever chooses you is an abomination.
text.Rotherham: Lo! ye, are, of nought, And your work is a puff of breath,—An abomination, he that chooseth you!
text.DRB: Behold, you are of nothing, and your work of that which hath no being: he that hath chosen you is an abomination.

text.NRSV: I stirred up one from the north, and he has come, from the rising of the sun he was summoned by name. He shall trample on rulers as on mortar, as the potter treads clay.
text.Rotherham: I have roused up one from the North, and he hath come, From the rising of the sun, calleth he on my Name,—And he hath come, on deputies, as though they were mortar, And as a potter treadeth clay.
text.DRB: I have raised up one from the north, and he shall come from the rising of the sun: he shall call upon my name, and he shall make princes to be as dirt, and as the potter treading clay.

text.NRSV: Who declared it from the beginning, so that we might know, and beforehand, so that we might say, “He is right”? There was no one who declared it, none who proclaimed, none who heard your words.
text.Rotherham: Who hath told in advance, that we might know, And beforetime, that we might say Right! Nay, there is none who can tell. Nay, there is none who can let us hear, Nay, there is none who can understand what ye utter.
text.DRB: Who hath declared from the beginning, that we may know: and from time of old, that we may say: Thou art just. There is none that sheweth, nor that foretelleth, nor that heareth your words.

text.NRSV: I first have declared it to Zion, and I give to Jerusalem a herald of good tidings.
text.Rotherham: He who is First, [can say], To Zion, Lo! there they are! And to Jerusalem, A herald of good-tidings, do I give.
text.DRB: The first shall say to Sion: Behold they are here, and to Jerusalem I will give an evangelist.

text.NRSV: But when I look there is no one; among these there is no counselor who, when I ask, gives an answer.
text.Rotherham: So I look, And there is not, a Man! Even among these And there is none to advise,—That, when I ask them, can answer a word.
text.DRB: And I saw, and there was no one even among them to consult, or who, when I asked, could answer a word.

text.NRSV: See, they are all a delusion; their works are nothing; their images are empty wind.
text.Rotherham: Lo! as to all of them, Vanity—nothingness, are their works, Wind and emptiness, their molten images!
text.DRB: Behold they are all in the wrong, and their works are vain: their idols are wind and vanity.

text.NRSV: Here is my servant, whom I uphold, my chosen, in whom my soul delights; I have put my spirit upon him; he will bring forth justice to the nations.
text.Rotherham: Lo! my Servant, I will uphold him, My chosen, well-pleased is my soul,—I have put my spirit upon him, Justice—to the nations, will he bring forth:
text.DRB: Behold my servant , I will uphold him: my elect, my soul delighteth in him: I have given my spirit upon him, he shall bring forth judgment to the Gentiles.

text.NRSV: He will not cry or lift up his voice, or make it heard in the street;
text.Rotherham: He will not cry out nor will he speak loud,—Nor cause to be heard, in the street, his voice:
text.DRB: He shall not cry, nor have respect to person, neither shall his voice be heard abroad.

text.NRSV: a bruised reed he will not break, and a dimly burning wick he will not quench; he will faithfully bring forth justice.
text.Rotherham: Cane that is crushed, will he not break, And wick that is fading, will he not quench,—Faithfully, will he bring forth justice:
text.DRB: The bruised reed he shall not break, and smoking flax he shall not quench: he shall bring forth judgment unto truth.

text.NRSV: He will not grow faint or be crushed until he has established justice in the earth; and the coastlands wait for his teaching.
text.Rotherham: He will not fade, nor will he be crushed, Until he establish, in the earth, justice, And for his instruction, Coastlands, wait.
text.DRB: He shall not be sad, nor troublesome, till he set judgment in the earth: and the islands shall wait for his law.

text.NRSV: Thus says God, the Lord, who created the heavens and stretched them out, who spread out the earth and what comes from it, who gives breath to the people upon it and spirit to those who walk in it:
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith GOD himself—Yahweh,—Creator of the heavens that stretched them forth, Out-spreader of earth, and the products thereof,—Giver of breath to the people thereon, And of spirit to them who walk therein,
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord God that created the heavens, and stretched them out: that established the earth, and the things that spring out of it: that giveth breath to the people upon it, and spirit to them that tread thereon.

text.NRSV: I am the Lord, I have called you in righteousness, I have taken you by the hand and kept you; I have given you as a covenant to the people, a light to the nations,
text.Rotherham: I—Yahweh, have called thee in righteousness, And will firmly grasp thy hand,—And will keep thee And give thee—As the covenant of a people, As the light of nations:
text.DRB: I the Lord have called thee in justice, and taken thee by the hand, and preserved thee. And I have given thee for a covenant of the people, for a light of the Gentiles:

text.NRSV: to open the eyes that are blind, to bring out the prisoners from the dungeon, from the prison those who sit in darkness.
text.Rotherham: To open eyes that are blind,—To bring forth Out of the dungeon the captive Out of the prison, the dwellers in darkness.
text.DRB: That thou mightest open the eyes of the blind, and bring forth the prisoner out of prison, and them that sit in darkness out of the prison house.

text.NRSV: I am the Lord, that is my name; my glory I give to no other, nor my praise to idols.
text.Rotherham: I, am Yahweh, that, is my Name, And, my glory, to another, will I not give, Nor my praise to images.
text.DRB: I the Lord, this is my name: I will not give my glory to another, nor my praise to graven things.

text.NRSV: See, the former things have come to pass, and new things I now declare; before they spring forth, I tell you of them.
text.Rotherham: Things told in advance, lo! they have come to pass,—And new things, am I telling, Ere yet they spring forth, I let, you, hear them.
text.DRB: The things that were first, behold they are come: and new things do I declare: before they spring forth, I will make you hear them.

text.NRSV: Sing to the Lord a new song, his praise from the end of the earth! Let the sea roar and all that fills it, the coastlands and their inhabitants.
text.Rotherham: Sing to Yahweh, a song that is new, His praise, from the end of the earth,—Ye that go down to the sea, and the fulness thereof, The Coastlands and ye who dwell therein.
text.DRB: Sing ye to the Lord a new song, his praise is from the ends of the earth: you that go down to the sea, and all that are therein: ye islands, and ye inhabitants of them.

text.NRSV: Let the desert and its towns lift up their voice, the villages that Kedar inhabits; let the inhabitants of Sela sing for joy, let them shout from the tops of the mountains.
text.Rotherham: Let the wilderness shout, and the cities thereof, The villages wherein dwelleth Kedar,—Let the inhabitants of the crag, raise shouts of triumph, From the top of the mountains, let them cry aloud:
text.DRB: Let the desert and the cities thereof be exalted: Cedar shall dwell in houses: ye inhabitants of Petra , give praise, they shall cry from the top of the mountains.

text.NRSV: Let them give glory to the Lord, and declare his praise in the coastlands.
text.Rotherham: Let them render unto Yahweh, glory,—And, his praise, in the Coastlands let them tell.
text.DRB: They shall give glory to the Lord, and shall declare his praise in the islands.

text.NRSV: The Lord goes forth like a soldier, like a warrior he stirs up his fury; he cries out, he shouts aloud, he shows himself mighty against his foes.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, as a hero, goeth forth, As a man of war, he stirreth up jealousy,—He giveth a cry, yea he raiseth a war-cry, Over his foes, he showeth his strength.
text.DRB: The Lord shall go forth as a mighty man, as a man of war shall he stir up zeal: he shall shout and cry: he shall prevail against his enemies.

text.NRSV: For a long time I have held my peace, I have been silent and restrained myself; now I will cry out like a woman in labor, I will gasp and pant.
text.Rotherham: I have held my peace from age-past times, I kept still, I restrained myself,—As a travailing woman, I pant, I breathe hard and gasp, all at once!
text.DRB: I have always held my peace, I have I kept silence, I have been patient, I will speak now as a woman in labour: I will destroy, and swallow up at once.

text.NRSV: I will lay waste mountains and hills, and dry up all their herbage; I will turn the rivers into islands, and dry up the pools.
text.Rotherham: I will lay waste mountains, and hills, And all their vegetation, will I wither,—And I will make rivers to be shores, And lakes, will I dry up:
text.DRB: I will lay waste the mountains and hills, and will make all their grass to wither: and I will turn rivers into islands, and will dry up the standing pools.

text.NRSV: I will lead the blind by a road they do not know, by paths they have not known I will guide them. I will turn the darkness before them into light, the rough places into level ground. These are the things I will do, and I will not forsake them.
text.Rotherham: Thus will I lead the blind, by a way they know not, In paths they know not, will I guide them,—I will make the place that was dark before them to be, light. And crooked ways, to be, straight, These things, have I done unto them, And have not forsaken them.
text.DRB: And I will lead the blind into the way which they know not: and in the paths which they were ignorant of I will make them walk: I will make darkness light before them, and crooked things straight: these things have I done to them, and have not forsaken them.

text.NRSV: They shall be turned back and utterly put to shame—those who trust in carved images, who say to cast images, “You are our gods.”
text.Rotherham: They have drawn back They turn very pale Who have been trusting in a graven image,—Who have been saying to a molten image, Ye, are our gods!
text.DRB: They are turned back: let them be greatly confounded, that trust in a graven thing, that say to a molten thing: You are our god.

text.NRSV: Listen, you that are deaf; and you that are blind, look up and see!
text.Rotherham: Ye deaf hear! And ye blind look around that ye may see,
text.DRB: Hear, ye deaf, and, ye blind, behold that you may see.

text.NRSV: Who is blind but my servant, or deaf like my messenger whom I send? Who is blind like my dedicated one, or blind like the servant of the Lord?
text.Rotherham: Who is blind if not my Servant? Or deaf, like, my messenger whom I send? Who is, blind, like an intimate friend? Or blind like the Servant of Yahweh?
text.DRB: Who is blind, but my servant? or deaf, but he to whom I have sent my messengers? Who is blind, but he that is sold? or who is blind, but the servant of the Lord?

text.NRSV: He sees many things, but does not observe them; his ears are open, but he does not hear.
text.Rotherham: Seeing many things, yet thou heedest not, Opening the ears yet he heareth not,
text.DRB: Thou that seest many things, wilt thou not observe them? thou that hast ears open, wilt thou not hear?

text.NRSV: The Lord was pleased, for the sake of his righteousness, to magnify his teaching and make it glorious.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, is well-pleased for his own righteousness’ sake, He magnifieth instruction and maketh it majestic.
text.DRB: And the Lord was willing to sanctify him, and to magnify the law, and exalt it.

text.NRSV: But this is a people robbed and plundered, all of them trapped in holes and hidden in prisons; they have become a prey with no one to rescue, a spoil with no one to say, “Restore!”
text.Rotherham: But, that, is a people preyed upon and plundered, Snared in holes, all of them, And, in houses of restraint, concealed,—They have become a prey, and there is none to deliver,—A booty, and there is none to say—Restore!
text.DRB: But this is a people that is robbed and wasted: they are all the snare of young men, and they are hid in the houses of prisons: they are made a prey, and there is none to deliver them: a spoil, and there is none that saith: Restore.

text.NRSV: Who among you will give heed to this, who will attend and listen for the time to come?
text.Rotherham: Who among you, will give ear to this,—Let him hearken and hear, for an aftertime?
text.DRB: Who is there among you that will give ear to this, that will attend and hearken for times to come?

text.NRSV: Who gave up Jacob to the spoiler, and Israel to the robbers? Was it not the Lord, against whom we have sinned, in whose ways they would not walk, and whose law they would not obey?
text.Rotherham: Who gave, as a booty, Jacob. And, Israel, to them who were ready to take prey? Was it not, Yahweh? He against whom we have sinned, And they were not willing, in his ways, to walk, Neither hearkened they to his instruction?
text.DRB: Who hath given Jacob for a spoil, and Israel to robbers? hath not the Lord himself, against whom we have sinned? And they would not walk in his ways, and they have not hearkened to his law.

text.NRSV: So he poured on him the heat of his anger and the fury of war; it set him on fire all around, but he did not understand; it burned him, but he did not take it to heart.
text.Rotherham: So he hath poured out, upon him, the glow of his anger, and the strength of battle; And it hath set him aflame round about, yet he knoweth it not, And it hath kindled upon him yet he layeth it not to heart.
text.DRB: And he hath poured out upon him the indignation of his fury, and a strong battle, and hath burnt him round about, and he knew not: and set him on fire, and he understood not.

text.NRSV: But now thus says the Lord, he who created you, O Jacob, he who formed you, O Israel: Do not fear, for I have redeemed you; I have called you by name, you are mine.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, Thus, saith Yahweh—Creating thee, O Jacob, and Fashioning thee O Israel,—Do not fear, For I have redeemed thee, I have called thee by thy name. Mine, thou art!
text.DRB: And now thus saith the Lord that created thee, O Jacob, and formed thee, O Israel: Fear not, for I have redeemed thee, and called thee by thy name: thou art mine.

text.NRSV: When you pass through the waters, I will be with you; and through the rivers, they shall not overwhelm you; when you walk through fire you shall not be burned, and the flame shall not consume you.
text.Rotherham: When thou passest through the waters, with thee, I am, Or, through the rivers, they shall not overflow thee,—When thou walkest through fire, thou shall not be scorched, And, a flame, shall not kindle upon thee;
text.DRB: When thou shalt pass through the waters, I will be with thee, and the rivers shall not cover thee: when thou shalt walk in the fire, thou shalt not be burnt, and the flames shall not burn in thee:

text.NRSV: For I am the Lord your God, the Holy One of Israel, your Savior. I give Egypt as your ransom, Ethiopia and Seba in exchange for you.
text.Rotherham: For, I,—Yahweh, am, Thy God, The Holy One of Israel Ready to save thee,—I have given, as thy ransom, Egypt, Ethiopia and Seba, in thy stead.
text.DRB: For I am the Lord thy God, the Holy One of Israel, thy Saviour: I have given Egypt for thy atonement, Ethiopia and Saba for thee.

text.NRSV: Because you are precious in my sight, and honored, and I love you, I give people in return for you, nations in exchange for your life.
text.Rotherham: Because thou art precious in mine eyes, art honoured And, I, love thee,—And will give mankind, in thy stead, Yea, peoples, for thy life,
text.DRB: Since thou becamest honourable in my eyes, thou art glorious: I have loved thee, and I will give men for thee, and people for thy life.

text.NRSV: Do not fear, for I am with you; I will bring your offspring from the east, and from the west I will gather you;
text.Rotherham: Do not fear for, with thee, I am,—From the East, will I bring in thy seed, And, from the West, will I gather thee:
text.DRB: Fear not, for I am with thee: I will bring thy seed from the east, and gather thee from the west.

text.NRSV: I will say to the north, “Give them up,” and to the south, “Do not withhold; bring my sons from far away and my daughters from the end of the earth—
text.Rotherham: I will say to the North Give up! And to the South Do not withhold! Bring in My sons from far, and My daughters from the end of the earth;
text.DRB: I will say to the north: Give up: and to the south: Keep not back: bring my sons from afar, and my daughters from the ends of the earth.

text.NRSV: everyone who is called by my name, whom I created for my glory, whom I formed and made.”
text.Rotherham: Every one who is called by my Name, Whom for mine own glory, I have created—formed—yea, made!
text.DRB: And every one that calleth upon my name, I have created him for my glory, I have formed him, and made him.

text.NRSV: Bring forth the people who are blind, yet have eyes, who are deaf, yet have ears!
text.Rotherham: Bring forth A blind people that have, eyes, and A deaf, that have, ears.
text.DRB: Bring forth the people that are blind, and have eyes: that are deaf, and have ears.

text.NRSV: Let all the nations gather together, and let the peoples assemble. Who among them declared this, and foretold to us the former things? Let them bring their witnesses to justify them, and let them hear and say, “It is true.”
