text.Rotherham: All the nations, are gathered together Yea there is an assembling of peoples, Who among them, can tell this, And things in advance, can let us hear? Let them set forth their witnesses that they may get their right, Or let them hear, and say Truth!
text.DRB: All the nations are assembled together, and the tribes are gathered: who among you can declare this, and shall make us hear the former things? let them bring forth their witnesses, let them be justified, and hear, and say: It is truth.

text.NRSV: You are my witnesses, says the Lord, and my servant whom I have chosen, so that you may know and believe me and understand that I am he. Before me no god was formed, nor shall there be any after me.
text.Rotherham: Ye, are my witnesses Declareth Yahweh, And my Servant, whom I have chosen,—That ye may take note—and believe me, And perceive that, I, am He, Before me, was not formed a GOD, Nor after me, shall one come into being:
text.DRB: You are my witnesses, saith the Lord, and my servant whom I have chosen: that you may know, and believe me, and understand that I myself am. Before me there was no God formed, and after me there shall be none.

text.NRSV: I, I am the Lord, and besides me there is no savior.
text.Rotherham: I—I, am Yahweh,—And there is none besides me ready to save:
text.DRB: I am, I am the Lord: and there is no saviour besides me.

text.NRSV: I declared and saved and proclaimed, when there was no strange god among you; and you are my witnesses, says the Lord. I am God,
text.Rotherham: I, have told—and will save, and make known, That there is among you no strange one; And, ye, are my witnesses, Declareth Yahweh That, I, am GOD.
text.DRB: I have declared, and have saved. I have made it heard, and there was no strange one among you. You are my witnesses, saith the Lord, and I am God.

text.NRSV: and also henceforth I am he; there is no one who can deliver from my hand; I work and who can hinder it?
text.Rotherham: Even from To-day, I, am He, And none, out of my hand, can deliver,—I work and who reverseth?
text.DRB: And from the beginning I am the same, and there is none that can deliver out of my hand: I will work, and who shall turn it away?

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: For your sake I will send to Babylon and break down all the bars, and the shouting of the Chaldeans will be turned to lamentation.
text.Rotherham: Thus saith Yahweh, Your Redeemer, The Holy One of Israel,—For your sakes, have I sent into Babylon, And will bring down as fugitives, all of them,—Even the Chaldeans, in the ships which cause them loud outcry.
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord your redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: For your sake I sent to Babylon, and have brought down all their bars, and the Chaldeans glorying in their ships.

text.NRSV: I am the Lord, your Holy One, the Creator of Israel, your King.
text.Rotherham: I—Yahweh, am Your Holy One,—The Creator of Israel, Your King.
text.DRB: I am the Lord your Holy One, the Creator of Israel, your King.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, who makes a way in the sea, a path in the mighty waters,
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh,—Who setteth in the sea, a way,—And in the mighty waters, a path;
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord, who made a way in the sea, and a path in the mighty waters.

text.NRSV: who brings out chariot and horse, army and warrior; they lie down, they cannot rise, they are extinguished, quenched like a wick:
text.Rotherham: Who bringeth forth Chariot and horse, Force and strength, Together, shall they lie down They shall not arise, They are extinguished Like a wick, are they quenched!
text.DRB: Who brought forth the chariot and the horse, the army and the strong: they lay down to sleep together, and they shall not rise again: they are broken as flax, and are extinct.

text.NRSV: Do not remember the former things, or consider the things of old.
text.Rotherham: Do not keep in mind former things,—And things of old, do not consider:
text.DRB: Remember not former things, and look not on things of old.

text.NRSV: I am about to do a new thing; now it springs forth, do you not perceive it? I will make a way in the wilderness and rivers in the desert.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! doing a new thing, Now, shall it spring forth, Will ye not take note thereof? Surely I will set In the desert, a way, In a desolate land, rivers:
text.DRB: Behold I do new things, and now they shall spring forth, verily you shall know them: I will make a way in the wilderness, and rivers in the desert.

text.NRSV: The wild animals will honor me, the jackals and the ostriches; for I give water in the wilderness, rivers in the desert, to give drink to my chosen people,
text.Rotherham: The wild beast of the field shall honour me, Jackals, and ostriches, Because I have given—In the desert, waters, Rivers in, desolate land, To give drink unto my people, my chosen:
text.DRB: The beast of the field shall glorify me, the dragons and the ostriches: because I have given waters in the wilderness, rivers in the desert, to give drink to my people, to my chosen.

text.NRSV: the people whom I formed for myself so that they might declare my praise.
text.Rotherham: A people which I have fashioned for myself, My praise, shall they record.
text.DRB: This people have I formed for myself, they shall shew forth my praise.

text.NRSV: Yet you did not call upon me, O Jacob; but you have been weary of me, O Israel!
text.Rotherham: Yet not upon me, hast thou called, O Jacob,—For thou hast been wearied of me O Israel:
text.DRB: But thou hast not called upon me, O Jacob, neither hast thou laboured about me, O Israel.

text.NRSV: You have not brought me your sheep for burnt offerings, or honored me with your sacrifices. I have not burdened you with offerings, or wearied you with incense.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast not brought in to me small cattle as thine ascending-offerings, Nor, with thy sacrifices, hast thou honoured me,—I have not oppressed thee with meal-offerings, Nor have I wearied thee with frankincense;
text.DRB: Thou hast not offered me the ram of thy holocaust, nor hast thou glorified me with thy victims: I have not caused thee to serve with oblations, nor wearied thee with incense.

text.NRSV: You have not bought me sweet cane with money, or satisfied me with the fat of your sacrifices. But you have burdened me with your sins; you have wearied me with your iniquities.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast not bought for me with silver, fragrant calamus, Nor with the fat of thy sacrifices, hast thou sated me,—Thou hast done nothing but oppress me with thy sins, Thou hast wearied me with thine iniquities.
text.DRB: Thou hast bought me no sweet cane with money, neither hast thou filled me with the fat of thy victims. But thou hast made me to serve with thy sins, thou hast wearied me with thy iniquities.

text.NRSV: I, I am he who blots out your transgressions for my own sake, and I will not remember your sins.
text.Rotherham: I—I, am he that is ready, To wipe out thy transgressions for mine own sake,—And, thy sins, not remember.
text.DRB: I am, I am he that blot out thy iniquities for my own sake, and I will not remember thy sins.

text.NRSV: Accuse me, let us argue together; set forth your case, so that you may be proved right.
text.Rotherham: Put me in mind, Let us enter into judgment at once,—Recount, thou, that thou mayest be justified:
text.DRB: Put me in remembrance, and let us plead together: tell if thou hast any thing to justify thyself.

text.NRSV: Your first ancestor sinned, and your interpreters transgressed against me.
text.Rotherham: Thy chief father, hath sinned, And thine interpreters, have transgressed against me;
text.DRB: Thy first father sinned, and thy teachers have transgressed against me.

text.NRSV: Therefore I profaned the princes of the sanctuary, and delivered Jacob to utter destruction and Israel to reviling.
text.Rotherham: So then I must needs profane the rulers of the holy place,—And deliver Jacob to be devoted to destruction, and Israel, unto reviling!
text.DRB: And I have profaned the holy princes, I have given Jacob to slaughter, and Israel to reproach.

text.NRSV: But now hear, O Jacob my servant, Israel whom I have chosen!
text.Rotherham: Now, then—hear, O Jacob, my Servant,—and Israel whom I have chosen:
text.DRB: And now hear, O Jacob, my servant, and Israel whom I have chosen.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord who made you, who formed you in the womb and will help you: Do not fear, O Jacob my servant, Jeshurun whom I have chosen.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh—Who made thee and formed thee from birth, Who helpeth thee: Do not fear O my Servant Jacob, and Jeshurun whom I have chosen;
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord that made and formed thee, thy helper from the womb: Fear not, O my servant Jacob, and thou most righteous whom I have chosen.

text.NRSV: For I will pour water on the thirsty land, and streams on the dry ground; I will pour my spirit upon your descendants, and my blessing on your offspring.
text.Rotherham: For I will pour, Water upon the thirsty soil, and Floods upon the dry ground,—I will pour My spirit upon thy seed, and My blessing upon thine offspring;
text.DRB: For I will pour out waters upon the thirsty ground, and streams upon the dry land: I will pour out my spirit upon thy seed, and my blessing upon thy stock.

text.NRSV: They shall spring up like a green tamarisk, like willows by flowing streams.
text.Rotherham: So will they spring up among the grass, As willows by the water-courses:
text.DRB: And they shall spring up among the herbs, as willows beside the running waters.

text.NRSV: This one will say, “I am the Lord’s,” another will be called by the name of Jacob, yet another will write on the hand, “The Lord’s,” and adopt the name of Israel.
text.Rotherham: This one, will say Yahweh’s, am I, and That one, will call himself by the name of Jacob, and Yonder one will write on his hand—Yahweh’s, And after the name of Israel, will one entitle himself.
text.DRB: One shall say: I am the Lord's, and another shall call himself by the name of Jacob, and another shall subscribe with his hand, To the Lord, and surname himself by the name of Israel.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, the King of Israel and his Redeemer, the Lord of hosts: I am the first and I am the last; besides me there is no god.
text.Rotherham: Thus saith Yahweh—King of Israel, Even his Redeemer, Yahweh of hosts,—I, am, First, and, I, Last, And besides me, there is no God.
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord the king of Israel, and his redeemer the Lord of hosts: I am the first, and I am the last, and besides me there is no God.

text.NRSV: Who is like me? Let them proclaim it, let them declare and set it forth before me. Who has announced from of old the things to come? Let them tell us what is yet to be.
text.Rotherham: Who then, like me, can call, and declare it, and order it, for me, Seeing that I appointed an age-abiding people,—Or things yet to be, and that shall come to pass, Let them declare on their part.
text.DRB: Who is like to me? let him call and declare: and let him set before me the order, since I appointed the ancient people: and the things to come, and that shall be hereafter, let them shew unto them.

text.NRSV: Do not fear, or be afraid; have I not told you from of old and declared it? You are my witnesses! Is there any god besides me? There is no other rock; I know not one.
text.Rotherham: Do not ye dread, nor yet be alarmed, Have I not from olden time, told thee and declared? So that, ye, are my witnesses,—Whether there is a GOD besides me? Or is no Rock—I knew of none!
text.DRB: Fear ye not, neither be ye troubled, from that time I have made thee to hear, and have declared: you are my witnesses. Is there a God besides me, a maker, whom I have not known?

text.NRSV: All who make idols are nothing, and the things they delight in do not profit; their witnesses neither see nor know. And so they will be put to shame.
text.Rotherham: The fashioners of an image—all of them, are emptiness, And, the things they delight in, cannot profit,—And, their, witnesses, they, neither see nor know, That they may be ashamed.
text.DRB: The makers of idols are all of them nothing, and their best beloved things shall not profit them. They are their witnesses, that they do not see, nor understand, that they may be ashamed.

text.NRSV: Who would fashion a god or cast an image that can do no good?
text.Rotherham: Who hath fashioned a GOD, Oran image, hath molten? It cannot profit!
text.DRB: Who hath formed a god, and made a graven thing that is profitable for nothing?

text.NRSV: Look, all its devotees shall be put to shame; the artisans too are merely human. Let them all assemble, let them stand up; they shall be terrified, they shall all be put to shame together.
text.Rotherham: Lo! all his partners, turn pale, Even, the artificers themselves, are of the sons of earth,—Let them gather themselves together—all of them. Let them take their stand, Let them dread, and turn pale, together!
text.DRB: Behold, all the partakers thereof shall be confounded: for the makers are men: they shall all assemble together, they shall stand and fear, and shall be confounded together.

text.NRSV: The ironsmith fashions it and works it over the coals, shaping it with hammers, and forging it with his strong arm; he becomes hungry and his strength fails, he drinks no water and is faint.
text.Rotherham: As for the smith, [with his] cutting-tool,—When he hath wrought in the live coals, And, with hammers, hath fashioned it,—And hath wrought it with his strong arm, Anon he is hungry, and hath no strength, He hath drunk no water and so hath become faint!
text.DRB: The smith hath wrought with his file, with coals, and with hammers he hath formed it, and hath wrought with the strength of his arm: he shall hunger and faint, he shall drink no water, and shall be weary.

text.NRSV: The carpenter stretches a line, marks it out with a stylus, fashions it with planes, and marks it with a compass; he makes it in human form, with human beauty, to be set up in a shrine.
text.Rotherham: As for the carpenter,—He hath stretched out a line hath drawn it with a pencil, Hath made it with carving tools, With compasses, hath rounded it,—And so hath made it after the figure of a great man, After the beauty of a son of earth, that it may remain in a house!
text.DRB: The carpenter hath stretched out his rule, he hath formed it with a plane: he hath made it with corners, and hath fashioned it round with the compass: and he hath made the image of a man as it were a beautiful man dwelling in a house.

text.NRSV: He cuts down cedars or chooses a holm tree or an oak and lets it grow strong among the trees of the forest. He plants a cedar and the rain nourishes it.
text.Rotherham: When one was cutting him down cedars, Then took he a holm-tree and an oak, And secured them for himself, among the trees of the forest,—He planted a fir-tree and the pouring rain made it grow;
text.DRB: He hath cut down cedars, taken the holm, and the oak that stood among the trees of the forest: he hath planted the pine tree, which the rain hath nourished.

text.NRSV: Then it can be used as fuel. If one takes part of it and warms oneself; one kindles a fire and bakes bread. Then one makes a god and worships it; makes it a carved image and bows down before it.
text.Rotherham: So it serveth for a man to burn, And he hath taken of the branches and warmed himself, Also he kindleth a fire, and baketh bread,—Also he maketh a GOD, and hath bowed himself down, Hath made of it a carved image, and adored it:
text.DRB: And it hath served men for fuel: he took thereof, and warmed himself: and he kindled it, and baked bread: but of the rest he made a god, and adored it: he made a graven thing, and bowed down before it.

text.NRSV: Half of it one burns in the fire; over this half one roasts meat, eats it and is satisfied. He also warms himself and says, “Ah, I am warm, I can feel the fire!”
text.Rotherham: The half thereof, hath he burned in the fire, Over half thereof, he eateth flesh, He roasteth roast, that he may be satisfied,—Also he warmeth himself, and saith, Aha, I am warm, I have seen a blaze;
text.DRB: Part of it he burnt with fire, and with part of it he dressed his meat: he boiled pottage, and was filled, and was warmed, and said: Aha, I am warm, I have seen the fire.

text.NRSV: The rest of it he makes into a god, his idol, bows down to it and worships it; he prays to it and says, “Deliver me, for you are my god!”
text.Rotherham: And the residue thereof, Into a GOD, he maketh, Into his carved image,—Adoreth it, and boweth down and prayeth unto it, And saith Deliver me, for, my GOD, thou art!
text.DRB: But the residue thereof he made a god, and a graven thing for himself: he boweth down before it, and adoreth it, and prayeth unto it, saying: Deliver me, for thou art my God.

text.NRSV: They do not know, nor do they comprehend; for their eyes are shut, so that they cannot see, and their minds as well, so that they cannot understand.
text.Rotherham: They have not taken note, neither can they perceive,—He hath besmeared—past seeing—their eyes, Past understanding, their hearts;
text.DRB: They have not known, nor understood: for their eyes are covered that they may not see, and that they may not understand with their heart.

text.NRSV: No one considers, nor is there knowledge or discernment to say, “Half of it I burned in the fire; I also baked bread on its coals, I roasted meat and have eaten. Now shall I make the rest of it an abomination? Shall I bow down before a block of wood?”
text.Rotherham: And no one reflecteth—There is neither knowledge nor discernment—to say, Half thereof, have I burned up in the fire Moreover also I have baked, on the coals thereof, bread, I roasted flesh, and have been eating,—And of the remainder thereof, an abomination, shall I make? And, to a log of wood, shall I pay adoration?
text.DRB: They do not consider in their mind, nor know, nor have the thought to say: I have burnt part of it in the fire, and I have baked bread upon the coals thereof: I have broiled flesh and have eaten, and of the residue thereof shall I make an idol? shall I fall down before the stock of a tree?

text.NRSV: He feeds on ashes; a deluded mind has led him astray, and he cannot deliver himself or say, “Is not this thing in my right hand a fraud?”
text.Rotherham: He is feeding on ashes, A deluded heart, hath turned him aside,—And he cannot deliver his own soul nor say, Is there not a falsehood in my right hand?
text.DRB: Part thereof is ashes: his foolish heart adoreth it, and he will not save his soul, nor say: Perhaps there is a lie in my right hand.

text.NRSV: Remember these things, O Jacob, and Israel, for you are my servant; I formed you, you are my servant; O Israel, you will not be forgotten by me.
text.Rotherham: Remember these things, O Jacob, And, Israel,—for, my Servant, thou art,—I have fashioned thee, a Servant of mine, thou art.
text.DRB: Remember these things, O Jacob, and Israel, for thou art my servant. I have formed thee, thou art my servant, O Israel, forget me not.

text.NRSV: I have swept away your transgressions like a cloud, and your sins like mist; return to me, for I have redeemed you.
text.Rotherham: O Israel, thou shalt not be forgotten of me, I have wiped out, As with a thick cloud, thy transgressions, And as with a broad cloud, thy sins,—Return unto me, for I have redeemed thee.
text.DRB: I have blotted out thy iniquities as a cloud, and thy sins as a mist: return to me, for I have redeemed thee.

text.NRSV: Sing, O heavens, for the Lord has done it; shout, O depths of the earth; break forth into singing, O mountains, O forest, and every tree in it! For the Lord has redeemed Jacob, and will be glorified in Israel.
text.Rotherham: Shout in triumph ye heavens for Yahweh, hath effectually wrought. Shout, O ye underparts of the earth, Ring out, Ye mountains, into cries of triumph, Thou forest, and every tree therein,—For, Yahweh, hath redeemed, Jacob, And in Israel, will he get himself glory.
text.DRB: Give praise, O ye heavens, for the Lord hath shewn mercy: shout with joy, ye ends of the earth: ye mountains, resound with praise, thou, O forest, and every tree therein: for the Lord hath redeemed Jacob, and Israel shall be glorified.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, your Redeemer, who formed you in the womb: I am the Lord, who made all things, who alone stretched out the heavens, who by myself spread out the earth;
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh Who hath redeemed thee, Who hath fashioned thee from birth,—I—Yahweh, am the maker of all things, Stretching out the heavens, alone, Spreading forth the earth, of myself;
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord thy redeemer, and thy maker, from the womb: I am the Lord, that make all things, that alone stretch out the heavens, that establish the earth, and there is none with me.

text.NRSV: who frustrates the omens of liars, and makes fools of diviners; who turns back the wise, and makes their knowledge foolish;
text.Rotherham: Frustrating the signs of praters, And, diviners, he confoundeth,—Turning wise men backwards, And their knowledge, he maketh folly;
text.DRB: That make void the tokens of diviners, and make the soothsayers mad. That turn the wise backward, and that, make their knowledge foolish.

text.NRSV: who confirms the word of his servant, and fulfills the prediction of his messengers; who says of Jerusalem, “It shall be inhabited,” and of the cities of Judah, “They shall be rebuilt, and I will raise up their ruins”;
text.Rotherham: Establishing the word of his Servant, And the counsel of his Messengers, he maketh good,—Who saith of Jerusalem—She shall be inhabited! And of the cities of Judah—They shall be built! And the ruins thereof, will I set up!
text.DRB: That raise up the word of my servant and perform the counsel of my messengers, who say to Jerusalem: Thou shalt be inhabited: and to the cities of Juda: You shall be built, and I will raise up the wastes thereof.

text.NRSV: who says to the deep, “Be dry— I will dry up your rivers”;
text.Rotherham: Who saith to the deep—Be dry and Thy rivers, will I drain!
text.DRB: Who say to the deep: Be thou desolate, and I will dry up thy rivers.

text.NRSV: who says of Cyrus, “He is my shepherd, and he shall carry out all my purpose”; and who says of Jerusalem, “It shall be rebuilt,” and of the temple, “Your foundation shall be laid.”
text.Rotherham: Who saith of Cyrus—My Shepherd! and All my pleasure, shall he make good Even saying of Jerusalem—She shall be built! And of the temple—Be her foundation laid!
text.DRB: Who say to Cyrus: Thou art my shepherd, and thou shalt perform all my pleasure. Who say to Jerusalem: Thou shalt be built: and to the temple: Thy foundations shall be laid.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord to his anointed, to Cyrus, whose right hand I have grasped to subdue nations before him and strip kings of their robes, to open doors before him— and the gates shall not be closed:
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh, to his Anointed, to Cyrus—Whose right hand I have firmly grasped To subdue before him, nations, And the loins of kings, will I ungird,—To open, before him, the two-leaved doors, And, the gates, shall not be shut:

text.NRSV: I will go before you and level the mountains, I will break in pieces the doors of bronze and cut through the bars of iron,
text.Rotherham: I, before thee, will go, And the hills, will I level—The doors of bronze, will I break in pieces, And the bars of iron, will I cut asunder;

text.NRSV: I will give you the treasures of darkness and riches hidden in secret places, so that you may know that it is I, the Lord, the God of Israel, who call you by your name.
text.Rotherham: Then will I give thee The treasures of darkness, Even the hoards of hidden places,—That thou mayest get to know That I—Yahweh, who am calling thee by thy name, am the God of Israel.

text.NRSV: For the sake of my servant Jacob, and Israel my chosen, I call you by your name, I surname you, though you do not know me.
text.Rotherham: For the sake of my servant Jacob, Even Israel my chosen Therefore have I called unto thee by thy name, I give thee a title though thou hast not known me,—

text.NRSV: I am the Lord, and there is no other; besides me there is no god. I arm you, though you do not know me,
text.Rotherham: I, am Yahweh, and there is none else, Besides me, there is no God,—I gird thee, though thou hast not known me:

text.NRSV: so that they may know, from the rising of the sun and from the west, that there is no one besides me; I am the Lord, and there is no other.
text.Rotherham: That men may get to know. From the rising of the sun And from the west. That there is none besides me,—I, am Yahweh, and there is none else:

text.NRSV: I form light and create darkness, I make spiritual well-being and create woe; I the Lord do all these things.
text.Rotherham: Forming light and creating darkness, Making prosperity, and creating misfortune,—I—Yahweh, who doeth all these.

text.NRSV: Shower, O heavens, from above, and let the skies rain down righteousness; let the earth open, that salvation may spring up, and let it cause righteousness to sprout up also; I the Lord have created it.
text.Rotherham: Let the drops fall ye heavens, from above, Yea, let, the skies, pour down righteousness,—Let the earth open and let them bear as their fruit—deliverance And let, justice, spring forth therewith, I—Yahweh, have created it.

text.NRSV: Woe to you who strive with your Maker, earthen vessels with the potter! Does the clay say to the one who fashions it, “What are you making?” or “Your work has no handles”?
text.Rotherham: Alas for him who contendeth with his Fashioner,—A potsherd, [should contend] with the potsherds of the ground! Shall it be said by the clay, unto him that is fashioning it, What wouldst thou make? Or, thy work say, of thee, He hath no hands?

text.NRSV: Woe to anyone who says to a father, “What are you begetting?” or to a mother, “With what are you in labor?”
text.Rotherham: Alas, for one who saith to a father, What begettest thou? Or to a woman What dost thou bring forth?

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, the Holy One of Israel, and its Maker: Will you question me about my children, or command me concerning the work of my hands?
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith, Yahweh, The Holy One of Israel And his Fashioner, As to things to come, they have asked me, Concerning my sons and concerning the work of my hands, they would command me!

text.NRSV: I made the earth, and created humankind upon it; it was my hands that stretched out the heavens, and I commanded all their host.
text.Rotherham: I, made the earth, And man upon it, I created,—I—mine own hands, stretched out the heavens, And all their host, I commanded:

text.NRSV: I have aroused Cyrus in righteousness, and I will make all his paths straight; he shall build my city and set my exiles free, not for price or reward, says the Lord of hosts.
text.Rotherham: I, have roused him up in righteousness, And all his roads, will I level,—He, shall build my city, And my captives, shall he let go, Not for price, nor for bribe, Saith Yahweh of hosts.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord: The wealth of Egypt and the merchandise of Ethiopia, and the Sabeans, tall of stature, shall come over to you and be yours, they shall follow you; they shall come over in chains and bow down to you. They will make supplication to you, saying, “God is with you alone, and there is no other; there is no god besides him.”
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh—The produce of Egypt and the gain of Ethiopia, and the Sabeans, men of great stature, Unto thee, shall come over, And thine, shall they become, After thee, shall they journey, In chains, shall they come over,—And unto thee, shall they bow down Unto thee, shall they pray [saying],—Surely, in thee, is a GOD And there is none else—no, God!

text.NRSV: Truly, you are a God who hides himself, O God of Israel, the Savior.
text.Rotherham: Surely, thou, art a GOD utterly hiding thyself,—O God of Israel, able to save!

text.NRSV: All of them are put to shame and confounded, the makers of idols go in confusion together.
text.Rotherham: They have turned pale and even been put to shame, all of them,—Together have they gone into disgrace, have the makers of images:

text.NRSV: But Israel is saved by the Lord with an everlasting salvation; you shall not be put to shame or confounded to all eternity.
text.Rotherham: Israel, hath been delivered by Yahweh, with an age-abiding deliverance,—Ye shall neither turn pale nor he put to shame, unto the ages of futurity,.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord, who created the heavens (he is God!), who formed the earth and made it (he established it; he did not create it a chaos, he formed it to be inhabited!): I am the Lord, and there is no other.
text.Rotherham: For, Thus, saith Yahweh, Who created the heavens God himself! Who fashioned the earth—And made it Himself, established it, …Not a waste, created he it To be dwelt in, he fashioned it, …I, am Yahweh, and there is none else:

text.NRSV: I did not speak in secret, in a land of darkness; I did not say to the offspring of Jacob, “Seek me in chaos.” I the Lord speak the truth, I declare what is right.
text.Rotherham: Not in secret, have I spoken In a place of the earth that is dark,—I have not said unto the seed of Jacob, In a waste, seek ye me,—I, am Yahweh, Speaking the thing that is right, Declaring the things that are just.

text.NRSV: Assemble yourselves and come together, draw near, you survivors of the nations! They have no knowledge— those who carry about their wooden idols, and keep on praying to a god that cannot save.
text.Rotherham: Assemble yourselves and come Draw near together, ye escaped of the nations,—They know not Who carry the wood of their carved image, And pray unto a GOD who cannot save.

text.NRSV: Declare and present your case; let them take counsel together! Who told this from of old, who declared it of old? Was it not I, the Lord? There is no other god besides me, a righteous God and a Savior; there is no one besides me.
text.Rotherham: Tell ye—and bring near, Yea let them take counsel, together,—Who let this be known aforetime. In time past, declared it? Was it not, I—Yahweh? And there is none else that is God besides me, A GOD, righteous and ready to save, There is none, besides me!

text.NRSV: Turn to me and be saved, all the ends of the earth! For I am God, and there is no other.
text.Rotherham: Turn unto me—and be ye saved, all ye ends of the earth,—For, I, am GOD, and there is none else.

text.NRSV: By myself I have sworn, from my mouth has gone forth in righteousness a word that shall not return: “To me every knee shall bow, every tongue shall swear.”
text.Rotherham: By myself, have I sworn, Gone forth out of my mouth, is righteousness as a decree And shall not turn back,—That, unto myself, Shall bow every knee, Shall swear every tongue:

text.NRSV: Only in the Lord, it shall be said of me, are righteousness and strength; all who were incensed against him shall come to him and be ashamed.
text.Rotherham: Only in Yahweh—for me, Hath one said, Is there righteousness and strength, Unto him, shall come and turn pale—All who have been incensed against him:

text.NRSV: In the Lord all the offspring of Israel shall triumph and glory.
text.Rotherham: In Yahweh, shall be justified and shall boast themselves—All the seed of Israel.

text.NRSV: Bel bows down, Nebo stoops, their idols are on beasts and cattle; these things you carry are loaded as burdens on weary animals.
text.Rotherham: Bel, hath crouched, Nebo, is cowering, Their images, are delivered up to beast and to cattle,—The things ye carried about, are become a load, A burden, to the weary!

text.NRSV: They stoop, they bow down together; they cannot save the burden, but themselves go into captivity.
text.Rotherham: They have cowered they have crouched at once, And they cannot rescue the burden,—But their own soul, into captivity, hath departed.

text.NRSV: Listen to me, O house of Jacob, all the remnant of the house of Israel, who have been borne by me from your birth, carried from the womb;
text.Rotherham: Hearken unto me O house of Jacob, Even all the remnant of the house of Israel,—Who have been borne from birth, Who have been carried from nativity:

text.NRSV: even to your old age I am he, even when you turn gray I will carry you. I have made, and I will bear; I will carry and will save.
text.Rotherham: Even unto old age, I, am, the same, And unto grey hairs, I, will bear the burden,—I have made and, I, will carry, Yea, I, will bear the burden and will deliver,

text.NRSV: To whom will you liken me and make me equal, and compare me, as though we were alike?
text.Rotherham: To whom can ye liken me or make me equal? Or compare me, and we be like?

text.NRSV: Those who lavish gold from the purse, and weigh out silver in the scales—they hire a goldsmith, who makes it into a god; then they fall down and worship!
text.Rotherham: They who pour gold out of a purse, And who weigh silver in a balance,—Who hire a goldsmith that he may make it into a GOD, They adore, yea they bow down;

text.NRSV: They lift it to their shoulders, they carry it, they set it in its place, and it stands there; it cannot move from its place. If one cries out to it, it does not answer or save anyone from trouble.
text.Rotherham: They carry him about on the shoulder They bear the burden of him—and set him in his place that he may stand,—Out of his place, will he not move,—Though one even make outcry unto him, he will not answer, Out of one’s trouble, he will not save him.

text.NRSV: Remember this and consider, recall it to mind, you transgressors,
text.Rotherham: Remember ye this and shew yourselves men,—Bring it back, ye transgressors, to your minds;

text.NRSV: remember the former things of old; for I am God, and there is no other; I am God, and there is no one like me,
text.Rotherham: Remember ye the things named in advance from age-past times,—For, I, am, The Mighty One, and there is none else, The Adorable and there is none like me!

text.NRSV: declaring the end from the beginning and from ancient times things not yet done, saying, “My purpose shall stand, and I will fulfill my intention,”
text.Rotherham: Declaring from the beginning, the latter end, And from olden time, that which had never been done,—Saying My purpose shall stand, and All my pleasure, will I perform;

text.NRSV: calling a bird of prey from the east, the man for my purpose from a far country. I have spoken, and I will bring it to pass; I have planned, and I will do it.
text.Rotherham: Calling, From the East, a Bird of Prey, From a far country, the Man I intended,—Yea I have spoken I will also bring it to pass, I have planned, I will also do it.

text.NRSV: Listen to me, you stubborn of heart, you who are far from deliverance:
text.Rotherham: Hearken unto me, Ye valiant of heart,—Who are far away from righteousness:

text.NRSV: I bring near my deliverance, it is not far off, and my salvation will not tarry; I will put salvation in Zion, for Israel my glory.
text.Rotherham: I have brought near my righteousness. It shall not be far away, And my deliverance, shall not linger,—But I will give In Zion, deliverance, To Israel, my glory.

text.NRSV: Come down and sit in the dust, virgin daughter Babylon! Sit on the ground without a throne, daughter Chaldea! For you shall no more be called tender and delicate.
text.Rotherham: Down—and sit in the dust, O virgin Daughter of Babylon, Sit on the ground—throneless, Daughter of the Chaldeans; For thou shalt no more be called Tender and Dainty.
text.DRB: Come down, sit in the dust, O virgin daughter of Babylon, sit on the ground: there is no throne for the daughter of the Chaldeans, for thou shalt no more be called delicate and tender.

text.NRSV: Take the millstones and grind meal, remove your veil, strip off your robe, uncover your legs, pass through the rivers.
text.Rotherham: Take millstones, and grind meal,—Put back thy veil—tuck up thy train Bare the leg, wade through streams:
text.DRB: Take a millstone and grind meal: uncover thy shame, strip thy shoulder, make bare thy legs, pass over the rivers.

text.NRSV: Your nakedness shall be uncovered, and your shame shall be seen. I will take vengeance, and I will spare no one.
text.Rotherham: Bared shall be thy shame, Yea seen thy reproach,—An avenging, will I take, And will accept no son of earth.
text.DRB: Thy nakedness shall be discovered, and thy shame shall be seen: I will take vengeance, and no man shall resist me.

text.NRSV: Our Redeemer—the Lord of hosts is his name—is the Holy One of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Our Redeemer, Yahweh of hosts, is his name! The Holy One of Israel.
text.DRB: Our redeemer, the Lord of hosts is his name, the Holy One of Israel.

text.NRSV: Sit in silence, and go into darkness, daughter Chaldea! For you shall no more be called the mistress of kingdoms.
text.Rotherham: Sit silent, and get into darkness, Daughter of the Chaldeans! For thou shalt no more be called Mistress of Kingdoms.
text.DRB: Sit thou silent, and get thee into darkness, O daughter of the Chaldeans: for thou shalt no more be called the lady of kingdoms.

text.NRSV: I was angry with my people, I profaned my heritage; I gave them into your hand, you showed them no mercy; on the aged you made your yoke exceedingly heavy.
text.Rotherham: I had been provoked with my people, Had profaned mine inheritance, And given them into thy hand, …Thou shewedst them no compassion, Upon the elder, madest thou very heavy thy yoke.
text.DRB: I was angry with my people, I have polluted my inheritance, and have given them into thy bend: thou hast shewn no mercy to them: upon the ancient thou hast laid thy yoke exceeding heavy.

text.NRSV: You said, “I shall be mistress forever,” so that you did not lay these things to heart or remember their end.
text.Rotherham: And thou saidst, Unto times age-abiding, shall I be Mistress,—Insomuch that thou laidst not these things to thy heart, Didst not keep in mind the issue thereof,
text.DRB: And thou hast said: I shall be a lady for ever: thou hast not laid these things to thy heart, neither hast thou remembered thy latter end.

text.NRSV: Now therefore hear this, you lover of pleasures, who sit securely, who say in your heart, “I am, and there is no one besides me; I shall not sit as a widow or know the loss of children” —
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore hear this, Thou Lady of pleasure Who dwelleth securely, Who saith in her heart,—I, [am], and there is no one besides, I shall not sit a widow, Nor know loss of children.
text.DRB: And now hear these things, thou that art delicate, and dwellest confidently, that sayest in thy heart: I am, and there is none else besides me: I shall not sit as a widow, and I shall not know barrenness.

text.NRSV: both these things shall come upon you in a moment, in one day: the loss of children and widowhood shall come upon you in full measure, in spite of your many sorceries and the great power of your enchantments.
text.Rotherham: Yet shall there come to thee—both these, in a moment, in one day, Loss of children and widowhood,—To their full, have they come on thee, Spite of the mass of thine incantations, Spite of the great throng of thy spells.
text.DRB: These two things shall come upon thee suddenly in one day, barrenness and widowhood. All things are come upon thee, because of the multitude of thy sorceries, and for the great hardness of thy enchanters.

text.NRSV: You felt secure in your wickedness; you said, “No one sees me.” Your wisdom and your knowledge led you astray, and you said in your heart, “I am, and there is no one besides me.”
text.Rotherham: And so thou didst trust in thy wickedness, Thou saidst, no one, seeth me, Thy wisdom and knowledge, the same, seduced thee,—Therefore saidst thou in thy heart, I [am], and there is no one besides.
text.DRB: And thou hast trusted in thy wickedness, and hast said: There is none that seeth me. Thy wisdom, and thy knowledge, this hath deceived thee. And thou hast said in thy heart: I am, and besides me there is no other.

text.NRSV: But evil shall come upon you, which you cannot charm away; disaster shall fall upon you, which you will not be able to ward off; and ruin shall come on you suddenly, of which you know nothing.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall come on thee—Mischief, Thou shalt not know how to charm it away Yea there shall fall on thee, Ruin, Thou shalt not be able to appease it,—And there shall come on thee suddenly. Desolation. Thou shalt not know.
text.DRB: Evil shall come upon thee, and then shalt not know the rising thereof: and calamity shall fall violently upon thee, which thou canst not keep off: misery shall come upon thee suddenly, which thou shalt not know.

text.NRSV: Stand fast in your enchantments and your many sorceries, with which you have labored from your youth; perhaps you may be able to succeed, perhaps you may inspire terror.
text.Rotherham: Take thy stand, I pray thee, With thy spells. And with the throng of thine incantations wherein thou hast wearied thyself from thy youth,—Peradventure thou mayest be able to profit Peradventure thou mayest strike me with terror.
text.DRB: Stand now with thy enchanters, and with the multitude of thy sorceries, in which thou hast laboured from thy youth, if so be it may profit thee any thing, or if thou mayst become stronger.

text.NRSV: You are wearied with your many counsels; let those who study the heavens stand up and save you, those who gaze at the stars, and at each new moon predict what shall befall you.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast worn thyself out with the mass of thy consultations,—Let them take their stand I pray thee that they may save thee—The dividers of the heavens—The gazers at the stars, They who make known by new moons, Somewhat of the things which shall come upon thee.
text.DRB: Thou hast failed in the multitude or thy counsels: let now the astrologers stand and save thee, they that gazed at the stars, and counted the months, that from them they might tell the things that shall come to thee.

text.NRSV: See, they are like stubble, the fire consumes them; they cannot deliver themselves from the power of the flame. No coal for warming oneself is this, no fire to sit before!
text.Rotherham: Lo! they have become as straw—a fire, hath burned them up, They shall not deliver their own soul from the grasp of the flame,—There is, no live coal to warm them, nor blaze to sit before.
text.DRB: Behold they are as stubble, fire hath burnt them, they shall not deliver themselves from the power of the flames: there are no coals wherewith they may be warmed, nor fire, that they may sit thereat.

text.NRSV: Such to you are those with whom you have labored, who have trafficked with you from your youth; they all wander about in their own paths; there is no one to save you.
text.Rotherham: Such, have they become to thee, with whom thou hast wearied thyself,—Thy merchants—from thy youth, will every man stagger straight onwards—There is none to save thee.
text.DRB: Such are all the things become to thee, in which thou hast laboured: thy merchants from thy youth, every one hath erred in his own way, there is none that can save thee.

text.NRSV: Hear this, O house of Jacob, who are called by the name of Israel, and who came forth from the loins of Judah; who swear by the name of the Lord, and invoke the God of Israel, but not in truth or right.
text.Rotherham: Hear ye this—O house of Jacob, Ye who call yourselves by the name of Israel, Yea from the waters of Judah, came they forth,—Who swear by the name of Yahweh, And, by the God of Israel, call to remembrance—Not in truth, nor in righteousness;
text.DRB: Hear ye these things, O house of Jacob, you that are called by the name of Israel, and are come forth out of the waters of Juda, you who swear by the name of the Lord, and make mention of the God of Israel, but not in truth, nor in justice.

text.NRSV: For they call themselves after the holy city, and lean on the God of Israel; the Lord of hosts is his name.
text.Rotherham: For of the holy city, do they call themselves, And on the God of Israel, do they stay themselves,—Yahweh of hosts, is his name.
text.DRB: For they are called of the holy city, and are established upon the God of Israel: the Lord of hosts is his name.

text.NRSV: The former things I declared long ago, they went out from my mouth and I made them known; then suddenly I did them and they came to pass.
text.Rotherham: Things in advance, of old, I declared, Yea out of mine own mouth, came they forth, that I might let them be known,—Suddenly, I wrought and they came to pass.
text.DRB: The former things of old I have declared, and they went forth out of my mouth, and I have made them to be heard: I did them suddenly and they came to pass.

text.NRSV: Because I know that you are obstinate, and your neck is an iron sinew and your forehead brass,
text.Rotherham: Because I knew that—Obstinate, thou wast,—And, a sinew of iron, was thy neck, And thy brow, brazen,
text.DRB: For I knew that thou art stubborn, and thy neck is as an iron sinew, and thy forehead as brass.

text.NRSV: I declared them to you from of old, before they came to pass I announced them to you, so that you would not say, “My idol did them, my carved image and my cast image commanded them.”
text.Rotherham: therefore told I thee—in time past, Ere yet it came, I let thee hear,—Lest thou shouldest say Mine idol wrought them, Yea, my carved image and my molten image, commanded them!
text.DRB: I foretold thee of old, before they came to pass I told thee, lest thou shouldst say: My idols have done these things, and my graven and molten things have commanded them.

text.NRSV: You have heard; now see all this; and will you not declare it? From this time forward I make you hear new things, hidden things that you have not known.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast heard—see it whole, And will, ye, not tell? I have let thee hear new things—from the present time, Even secrets which ye knew not.
text.DRB: See now all the things which thou hast heard: but have you declared them? I have shewn thee new things from that time, and things are kept which thou knowest not:

text.NRSV: They are created now, not long ago; before today you have never heard of them, so that you could not say, “I already knew them.”
text.Rotherham: Now, are they created, and not in time past, And, before to-day, thou hadst not heard of them,—Lest thou shouldest say, Lo! I knew them!
text.DRB: They are created now, and not of old: and before the day, when thou heardest them not, lest thou shouldst say: Behold I knew them.

text.NRSV: You have never heard, you have never known, from of old your ear has not been opened. For I knew that you would deal very treacherously, and that from birth you were called a rebel.
text.Rotherham: Nay! thou hadst not heard, Nay! thou hadst not known, Nay! in time past, thine ear, was not opened,—For I knew that thou, wouldst be treacherous, Yea a transgressor from birth, hast thou been called.
text.DRB: Thou hast neither heard, nor known, neither was thy ear opened of old. For I know that transgressing thou wilt transgress, and I have called thee a transgressor from the womb.

text.NRSV: For my name’s sake I defer my anger, for the sake of my praise I restrain it for you, so that I may not cut you off.
text.Rotherham: For the sake of mine own Name, will I defer mine anger, And for my praise, will I restrain myself towards thee,—So as not to cut thee off.
text.DRB: For my name's sake I will remove my wrath far off: and for my praise I will bridle thee, lest thou shouldst perish.

text.NRSV: See, I have refined you, but not like silver; I have tested you in the furnace of adversity.
text.Rotherham: Lo! I have refined thee, but not as silver,—I have tested thee, in a smelting-pot of affliction.
text.DRB: Behold I have refined thee, but not as silver, I have chosen thee in the furnace of poverty.

text.NRSV: For my own sake, for my own sake, I do it, for why should my name be profaned? My glory I will not give to another.
text.Rotherham: For mine own sake—for mine own sake, will I effectually work For how should it be profaned? And, my glory—to another, will I not give.
text.DRB: For my own sake, for my own sake will I do it, that I may not be blasphemed: and I will not give my glory to another.

text.NRSV: Listen to me, O Jacob, and Israel, whom I called: I am he; I am the first, and I am the last.
text.Rotherham: Hearken unto me O Jacob, And Israel, my called one,—I, am, the Same I, the first, yea, I, the last:
text.DRB: Hearken to me, O Jacob, and thou Israel whom I call: I am he, I am the first, and I am the last.

text.NRSV: My hand laid the foundation of the earth, and my right hand spread out the heavens; when I summon them, they stand at attention.
text.Rotherham: Surely, mine own hand, founded the earth, And, my right hand, stretched out the heavens,—While I was calling unto them, they stood forth, at once
text.DRB: My hand also hath founded the earth, and my right hand hath measured the heavens: I shall call them, and they shall stand together.

text.NRSV: Assemble, all of you, and hear! Who among them has declared these things? The Lord loves him; he shall perform his purpose on Babylon, and his arm shall be against the Chaldeans.
text.Rotherham: Assemble yourselves—all of you and hear, Who among them, hath told these things? he whom Yahweh loveth, will execute—His pleasure, on Babylon, And his stroke on the Chaldeans.
text.DRB: Assemble yourselves together, all you, and hear: who among them hath declared these things? the Lord hath loved him, he will do his pleasure in Babylon, and his arm shall be on the Chaldeans.

text.NRSV: I, even I, have spoken and called him, I have brought him, and he will prosper in his way.
text.Rotherham: I—I, have spoken, Yea I have called him,—I have brought him in, and he shall make prosperous his way.
text.DRB: I, even I have spoken and called him: I have brought him, and his way is made prosperous.

text.NRSV: Draw near to me, hear this! From the beginning I have not spoken in secret, from the time it came to be I have been there. And now the Lord God has sent me and his spirit.
text.Rotherham: Draw ye near unto me—hear ye this, Not in advance in secret, have I spoken, From the very time it cometh into being, there, am I,—And, now, My Lord Yahweh hath sent me and his spirit.
text.DRB: Come ye near unto me, and hear this: I have not spoken in secret from the beginning: from the time before it was done, I was there, and now the Lord God hath sent me, and his spirit.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: I am the Lord your God, who teaches you for your own good, who leads you in the way you should go.
text.Rotherham: Thus saith Yahweh—Thy Redeemer, The Holy One of Israel,—I—Yahweh, am thy God, Teaching thee to profit, Guiding thee in the way thou shouldest go.
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord thy redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: I am the Lord thy God that teach thee profitable things, that govern thee in the way that thou walkest.

text.NRSV: O that you had paid attention to my commandments! Then your prosperity would have been like a river, and your success like the waves of the sea;
text.Rotherham: Oh! that thou hadst hearkened to my commandments,—Then had been, like a river, thy prosperity, And, thy righteousness, like the waves of the sea:
text.DRB: O that thou hadst hearkened to my commandments: thy peace had been as a river, and thy justice as the waves of the sea,

text.NRSV: your offspring would have been like the sand, and your descendants like its grains; their name would never be cut off or destroyed from before me.
text.Rotherham: Then had been, like the sand, thy seed, And, the offspring of thy body, like the grains thereof,—Neither cut off nor destroyed had been his name from before me.
text.DRB: And thy seed had been as the sand, and the offspring of thy bowels like the gravel thereof: his name should not have perished, nor have been destroyed from before my face.

text.NRSV: Go out from Babylon, flee from Chaldea, declare this with a shout of joy, proclaim it, send it forth to the end of the earth; say, “The Lord has redeemed his servant Jacob!”
text.Rotherham: Come ye forth out of Babylon, Flee from among the Chaldeans, With triumphant voice, tell ye—let this be heard, Let it go forth as far as the end of the earth,—Say ye—Yahweh hath redeemed his servant Jacob!
text.DRB: Come forth out of Babylon, flee ye from the Chaldeans, declare it with the voice of joy: make this to be heard, and speak it out even to the ends of the earth. Say: The Lord hath redeemed his servant Jacob.

text.NRSV: They did not thirst when he led them through the deserts; he made water flow for them from the rock; he split open the rock and the water gushed out.
text.Rotherham: And they thirsted not, when, through dry places, he led them, Waters out of the rock, caused he to flow out to them,—Yea be cleft a rock and, there gushed out waters:
text.DRB: They thirsted not in the desert, when he led them out: he brought forth water out of the rock for them, and he clove the rock, and the waters gushed out.

text.NRSV: “There is no peace,” says the Lord, “for the wicked.”
text.Rotherham: No well-being, saith Yahweh to the lawless,
text.DRB: There is no peace to the wicked, saith the Lord.

text.NRSV: Listen to me, O coastlands, pay attention, you peoples from far away! The Lord called me before I was born, while I was in my mother’s womb he named me.
text.Rotherham: Hearken, ye Coastlands, unto me, And give ear ye peoples afar off,—Yahweh, called me, from my birth, From my nativity, made he mention of my name;
text.DRB: Give ear, ye islands, and hearken, ye people from afar. The Lord hath called me from the womb, from the bowels of my mother he hath been mindful of my name.

text.NRSV: He made my mouth like a sharp sword, in the shadow of his hand he hid me; he made me a polished arrow, in his quiver he hid me away.
text.Rotherham: And he made my mouth, like a sharp sword, In the shadow of his hand, he concealed me,—And made of me a polished arrow, In his quiver, he hid me;
text.DRB: And he hath made my mouth like a sharp sword: in the shadow of his hand he hath protected me, and hath made me as a chosen arrow: in his quiver he hath hidden me.

text.NRSV: And he said to me, “You are my servant, Israel, in whom I will be glorified.”
text.Rotherham: And said to me My Servant, thou art,—Israel, in whom I will get myself glory.
text.DRB: And he said to me: Thou art my servant Israel, for in thee will I glory.

text.NRSV: But I said, “I have labored in vain, I have spent my strength for nothing and vanity; yet surely my cause is with the Lord, and my reward with my God.”
text.Rotherham: But I, said, To no purpose, have I toiled, For waste and mist—my vigour, have I spent,—Surely, my vindication, is, with, Yahweh, And, my recompence, with my God.
text.DRB: And I said: I have laboured in vain, I have spent my strength without cause and in vain: therefore my judgment is with the Lord, and my work with my God.

text.NRSV: And now the Lord says, who formed me in the womb to be his servant, to bring Jacob back to him, and that Israel might be gathered to him, for I am honored in the sight of the Lord, and my God has become my strength —
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, said Yahweh—Fashioning me from birth To be Servant to him, To restore Jacob unto him, And that, Israel, unto him, might be gathered And I be honourable in the eyes of Yahweh, And, my God, be proved to have been my strength,
text.DRB: And now saith the Lord, that formed me from the womb to be his servant, that I may bring back Jacob unto him, and Israel will not be gathered together: and I am glorified in the eyes of the Lord, and my God is made my strength.

text.NRSV: he says, “It is too light a thing that you should be my servant to raise up the tribes of Jacob and to restore the survivors of Israel; I will give you as a light to the nations, that my salvation may reach to the end of the earth.”
text.Rotherham: Yea he said—It is too small a thing, for being my Servant, That thou shouldest raise up the tribes of Jacob, And the preserved of Israel, shouldst restore,—So I will give thee to become a light of nations, That, my salvation, may reach as far as the end of the earth.
text.DRB: And he said: It is a small thing that thou shouldst be my servant to raise up the tribes of Jacob, and to convert the dregs of Israel. Behold, I have given thee to be the light of the Gentiles, that thou mayst be my salvation even to the farthest part of the earth.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, the Redeemer of Israel and his Holy One, to one deeply despised, abhorred by the nations, the slave of rulers: “Kings shall see and stand up; princes, and they shall bow down, because of the Lord, who is faithful, the Holy One of Israel, who has chosen you.”
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh—the Redeemer of Israel, his Holy One To him that is despised of the soul, To the abhorred of the nation To the servant of rulers, Kings, shall see and arise, Princes, lo! they have bowed themselves down,—Because of Yahweh, who is faithful, The Holy One of Israel, lo! he hath chosen thee.
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord the redeemer of Israel, his Holy One, to the soul that is despised, to the nation that is abhorred, to the servant of rulers: Kings shall see, and princes shall rise up, and adore for the Lord's sake, because he is faithful, and for the Holy One of Israel, who hath chosen thee.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord: In a time of favor I have answered you, on a day of salvation I have helped you; I have kept you and given you as a covenant to the people, to establish the land, to apportion the desolate heritages;
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh—In a time of acceptance, have I answered thee, In a day of salvation, have I helped thee,—That I may preserve thee And give thee as the covenant of a people, To establish the land, To bring into possession the desolate heritages;
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord: In an acceptable time I have heard thee, and in the day of salvation I have helped thee: and I have preserved thee, and given thee to be a covenant of the people, that thou mightest raise up the earth, and possess the inheritances that were destroyed:

text.NRSV: saying to the prisoners, “Come out,” to those who are in darkness, “Show yourselves.” They shall feed along the ways, on all the bare heights shall be their pasture;
text.Rotherham: Saying To them who are bound Go forth, To them who are in darkness, Shew yourselves,—By the roads, shall they graze, And on all bare places, shall be their pasture:
text.DRB: That thou mightest say to them that are bound: Come forth: and to them that are in darkness: shew yourselves. They shall feed in the ways, and their pastures shall be in every plain.

text.NRSV: they shall not hunger or thirst, neither scorching wind nor sun shall strike them down, for he who has pity on them will lead them, and by springs of water will guide them.
text.Rotherham: They shall neither hunger, nor thirst, Nor shall smite them the glowing sand, or the glaring sun,—For, he that hath compassion upon them, will lead them, And unto springs of water, will he conduct them.
text.DRB: They shall not hunger, nor thirst, neither shall the heat nor the sun strike them: for he that is merciful to them, shall be their shepherd, and at the fountains of waters he shall give them drink.

text.NRSV: And I will turn all my mountains into a road, and my highways shall be raised up.
text.Rotherham: Then will I make of all my mountains, a road, And my highways, shall be upraised.
text.DRB: And I will make all my mountains a way, and my paths shall be exalted.

text.NRSV: Lo, these shall come from far away, and lo, these from the north and from the west, and these from the land of Syene.
text.Rotherham: Lo! these, from afar, shall come in,—And lo! these, from the North and from the West, And these, from the land of Sinim.
text.DRB: Behold these shall come from afar, and behold these from the north and from the sea, and these from the south country.

text.NRSV: Sing for joy, O heavens, and exult, O earth; break forth, O mountains, into singing! For the Lord has comforted his people, and will have compassion on his suffering ones.
text.Rotherham: Shout in triumph—O heavens! And exult—O earth! And break forth, ye mountains, into shouts of triumph,—For Yahweh hath comforted his people, And on his humbled ones, taketh he compassion.
text.DRB: Give praise, O ye heavens, and rejoice, O earth, ye mountains, give praise with jubilation: because the Lord hath comforted his people, and will have mercy on his poor ones.

text.NRSV: But Zion said, “The Lord has forsaken me, my Lord has forgotten me.”
text.Rotherham: But Zion had said. Yahweh hath forsaken me,—Even, My Lord, hath forgotten me!
text.DRB: And Sion said: The Lord hath forsaken me, and the Lord hath forgotten me.

text.NRSV: Can a woman forget her nursing child, or show no compassion for the child of her womb? Even these may forget, yet I will not forget you.
text.Rotherham: Can, a woman, forget, her sucking child, Past taking compassion on the son of her womb? Even these, may forget, Yet will, I, not forget, thee:
text.DRB: Can a woman forget her infant, so as not to have pity on the son of her womb? and if she should forget, yet will not I forget thee.

text.NRSV: See, I have inscribed you on the palms of my hands; your walls are continually before me.
text.Rotherham: Lo! on the palms of my hands, have I portrayed thee,—Thy walls are before me continually.
text.DRB: Behold, I have graven thee in my hands: thy walls are always before my eyes.

text.NRSV: Your builders outdo your destroyers, and those who laid you waste go away from you.
text.Rotherham: In haste are thy sons,—They who have been tearing thee down and laying thee waste, out of thee, let them go forth!
text.DRB: Thy builders are come: they that destroy thee and make thee waste shall go out of thee.

text.NRSV: Lift up your eyes all around and see; they all gather, they come to you. As I live, says the Lord, you shall put all of them on like an ornament, and like a bride you shall bind them on.
text.Rotherham: Lift up, round about, thine eyes and see, All those, have gathered themselves together—have come to thee! As I live, Declareth Yahweh,—Surely all those as an ornament, shalt thou put on, And bind them about thee for a girdle as a bride.
text.DRB: Lift up thy eyes round about, and see all these are gathered together, they are come to thee: I live, saith the Lord, thou shalt be clothed with all these as with an ornament, and as a bride thou shalt put them about thee.

text.NRSV: Surely your waste and your desolate places and your devastated land—surely now you will be too crowded for your inhabitants, and those who swallowed you up will be far away.
text.Rotherham: Surely, as for thy wastes, and thy desolations, and thy land of ruins, Surely, now, shalt thou be too strait for thine inhabitants, And, far off, shall be they who have been swallowing thee up.
text.DRB: For thy deserts, and thy desolate places, and the land of thy destruction shall now be too narrow by reason of the inhabitants, and they that swallowed thee up shall be chased far away.

text.NRSV: The children born in the time of your bereavement will yet say in your hearing: “The place is too crowded for me; make room for me to settle.”
text.Rotherham: The children of whom thou wast bereaved shall yet say in thine ears,—Too strait for me, is the place Make room for me that I may settle down.
text.DRB: The children of thy barrenness shall still say in thy ears: The place is too strait for me, make me room to dwell in.

text.NRSV: Then you will say in your heart, “Who has borne me these? I was bereaved and barren, exiled and put away—so who has reared these? I was left all alone—where then have these come from?”
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou say in thy heart,—Who hath borne me, these, Seeing, I, have been bereaved and unfruitful, a captive and banished? These, therefore, who hath brought them up? Lo! I, was left, alone, These, where were they?
text.DRB: And thou shalt-say in thy heart: Who hath begotten these? I was barren and brought not forth, led away, and captive: and who hath brought up these? I was destitute and alone: and these, where were they?

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God: See, I will lift up my hand to the nations, and raise my signal to the peoples; and they shall bring your sons in their bosom, and your daughters shall be carried on their shoulders.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith My Lord, Yahweh—Lo! I will lift up, unto nations my hand, Yea unto peoples, will I raise high my banner,—And they shall bring in thy sons in their bosom, And, thy daughters, on the shoulder shall be borne;
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord God: Behold I will lift up my hand to the Gentiles, and will set up my standard to the people. And they shall bring thy sons in their arms, and carry thy daughters upon their shoulders.

text.NRSV: Kings shall be your foster fathers, and their queens your nursing mothers. With their faces to the ground they shall bow down to you, and lick the dust of your feet. Then you will know that I am the Lord; those who wait for me shall not be put to shame.
text.Rotherham: And, kings, shall be, thy foster-fathers. And their queens, thy nursing mothers, With face to the ground, shall they bow down to thee, The dust of thy feet, shall they lick,—So shalt thou know that, I, am Yahweh, In that, they shall not be put to shame, Who were waiting for me.
text.DRB: And kings shall be thy nursing fathers, and queens thy nurses: they shall worship thee with their face toward the earth, and they shall lick up the dust of thy feet. And thou shalt know that I am the Lord, for they shall not be confounded that wait for him.

text.NRSV: Can the prey be taken from the mighty, or the captives of a tyrant be rescued?
text.Rotherham: Shall prey, be taken from a mighty one? Or the captive of one in the right, be delivered?
text.DRB: Shall the prey be taken from the strong? or can that which was taken by the mighty be delivered?

text.NRSV: But thus says the Lord: Even the captives of the mighty shall be taken, and the prey of the tyrant be rescued; for I will contend with those who contend with you, and I will save your children.
text.Rotherham: Surely, thus, saith Yahweh—Even the captive of the mighty one, shall be taken away, And the prey of the tyrant, be delivered; And thine opposers, I, will oppose, And thy children, I, will save;
text.DRB: For thus saith the Lord: Yea verily, even the captivity shall be taken away from the strong: and that which was taken by the mighty, shall be delivered. But I will judge those that have judged thee, and thy children I will save.

text.NRSV: I will make your oppressors eat their own flesh, and they shall be drunk with their own blood as with wine. Then all flesh shall know that I am the Lord your Savior, and your Redeemer, the Mighty One of Jacob.
text.Rotherham: Then will I feed thy tormentors with their own flesh, And as with new wine—with their own blood, shall they be drunk,—So shall all flesh, know, that, I—Yahweh, am thy Saviour, And, thy Redeemer, is the Mighty One of Jacob.
text.DRB: And I will feed thy enemies with their own flesh: and they shall be made drunk with their own blood, as with new wine: and all flesh shall know, that I am the Lord that save thee, and thy Redeemer the Mighty One of Jacob.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord: Where is your mother’s bill of divorce with which I put her away? Or which of my creditors is it to whom I have sold you? No, because of your sins you were sold, and for your transgressions your mother was put away.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh—Where then is the scroll of your mother’s divorce, whom I have put away? Or, which of my creditors, is it to whom I have sold you? Lo! for your iniquities, have ye been sold, And for your transgressions, hath your mother been put away.
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord: What is this bill of the divorce of your mother, with which I have put her away? or who is my creditor, to whom I sold you: behold you are sold for your iniquities, and for your wicked deeds have I put your mother away.

text.NRSV: Why was no one there when I came? Why did no one answer when I called? Is my hand shortened, that it cannot redeem? Or have I no power to deliver? By my rebuke I dry up the sea, I make the rivers a desert; their fish stink for lack of water, and die of thirst.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, When I came in, was there no one? When I called, was there none to answer? Is mine own hand, really shortened, that it cannot redeem? Or is there not, in me, strength to deliver? Lo! by my rebuke, I dry up the sea I make rivers a desert, Their fish Stinketh, for want of water, Yea dieth, for thirst:
text.DRB: Because I came, and there was not a man: I called, and there was none that would hear. Is my hand shortened and become little, that I cannot redeem? or is there no strength in me to deliver? Behold at my rebuke I will make the sea a desert, I will turn the rivers into dry land: the fishes shall rot for want of water, and shall die for thirst.

text.NRSV: I clothe the heavens with blackness, and make sackcloth their covering.
text.Rotherham: I clothe the heavens with gloom, And, of sackcloth, make I their covering.
text.DRB: I will clothe the heavens with darkness, and will make sackcloth their covering.

text.NRSV: The Lord God has given me the tongue of a teacher, that I may know how to sustain the weary with a word. Morning by morning he wakens—wakens my ear to listen as those who are taught.
text.Rotherham: My Lord Yahweh, hath given unto me the tongue of the instructed, That I should know how to succour the fainting, with discourse,—He kept wakening—morning by morning. He kept wakening mine ear, to hearken as do the instructed;
text.DRB: The Lord hath given me a learned tongue, that I should know how to uphold by word him that is weary: he wakeneth in the morning, in the morning he wakeneth my ear, that I may hear him as a master.

text.NRSV: The Lord God has opened my ear, and I was not rebellious, I did not turn backward.
text.Rotherham: My Lord, Yahweh, opened mine ear, And, I, was not rebellious,—Away, turned I not:
text.DRB: The Lord God hath opened my ear, and I do not resist: I have not gone back.

text.NRSV: I gave my back to those who struck me, and my cheeks to those who pulled out the beard; I did not hide my face from insult and spitting.
text.Rotherham: My back, gave I to smiters, and My cheeks, to them who pulled out the beard,—My face, hid I not from insult and spitting.
text.DRB: I have given my body to the strikers, and my cheeks to them that plucked them: I have not turned away my face from them that rebuked me, and spit upon me.

text.NRSV: The Lord God helps me; therefore I have not been disgraced; therefore I have set my face like flint, and I know that I shall not be put to shame;
text.Rotherham: Because, My Lord, Yahweh, would help me, Therefore was I not deterred by insult,—Therefore did I set my face like flint, And I knew that I should not be put to shame.
text.DRB: The Lord God is my helper, therefore am I not confounded: therefore have I set my face as a most hard rock, and I know that I shall not be confounded.

text.NRSV: he who vindicates me is near. Who will contend with me? Let us stand up together. Who are my adversaries? Let them confront me.
text.Rotherham: At hand, is one who can justify me. Who will contend with me? let, us stand forth together,—Who can accuse me? let him draw near to me!
text.DRB: He is near that justifieth me, who will contend with me? let us stand together, who is my adversary? let him come near to me.

text.NRSV: It is the Lord God who helps me; who will declare me guilty? All of them will wear out like a garment; the moth will eat them up.
text.Rotherham: Lo! My Lord, Yahweh, will help me, Who is he that shall prove me lawless? Lo! they all, as a garment, shall fall to pieces, the moth shall consume them.
text.DRB: Behold the Lord God is my helper: who is he that shall condemn me? Lo, they shall all be destroyed as a garment, the moth shall eat them up.

text.NRSV: Who among you fears the Lord and obeys the voice of his servant, who walks in darkness and has no light, yet trusts in the name of the Lord and relies upon his God?
text.Rotherham: Who among you, revereth Yahweh, Hearkening unto the voice of his Servant,—That hath walked in dark places And hath had no gleam of light? Let him trust in the name of Yahweh, And lean upon his God.
text.DRB: Who is there among you that feareth the Lord, that heareth the voice of his servant, that hath walked in darkness, and hath no light? let him hope in the name of the Lord, and lean upon his God.

text.NRSV: But all of you are kindlers of fire, lighters of firebrands. Walk in the flame of your fire, and among the brands that you have kindled! This is what you shall have from my hand: you shall lie down in torment.
text.Rotherham: Lo! all ye that kindle a fire, That gird yourselves with fiery darts,—Walk ye in the blaze of your own fire. And in the fiery darts ye have kindled, At my hand, hath this befallen you, In sorrow, shall ye lie down.
text.DRB: Behold all you that kindle a fire, encompassed with flames, walk in the light of your fire, and in the flames which you have kindled: this is done to you by my hand, you shall sleep in sorrows.

text.NRSV: Listen to me, you that pursue righteousness, you that seek the Lord. Look to the rock from which you were hewn, and to the quarry from which you were dug.
text.Rotherham: Hearken unto me, Ye that pursue righteousness, Ye that seek Yahweh,—Look well unto the rock whence ye were hewn, And unto the quarry whence ye were digged:
text.DRB: Give ear to me, you that follow that which is just, and you that seek the Lord: look unto the rock whence you are hewn, and to the hole of the pit from which you are dug out.

text.NRSV: Look to Abraham your father and to Sarah who bore you; for he was but one when I called him, but I blessed him and made him many.
text.Rotherham: Look well unto Abraham your father, And unto Sarah who gave you birth,—For he was, alone, when called I him, And, I blessed him that I might make him, many.
text.DRB: Look unto Abraham your father, and to Sara that bore you: for I called him alone, and blessed him, and multiplied him.

text.NRSV: For the Lord will comfort Zion; he will comfort all her waste places, and will make her wilderness like Eden, her desert like the garden of the Lord; joy and gladness will be found in her, thanksgiving and the voice of song.
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh hath comforted Zion, He hath comforted all her waste places, And hath made her wilderness like Eden, And her waste plain like the garden of Yahweh,—Joy and gladness, shall be found in her, Thanksgiving, and the voice of melody.
text.DRB: The Lord therefore will comfort Sion, and will comfort all the ruins thereof: and he will make her desert as a place of pleasure, and her wilderness as the garden of the Lord. Joy and gladness shall be found therein, thanksgiving, and the voice of praise.

text.NRSV: Listen to me, my people, and give heed to me, my nation; for a teaching will go out from me, and my justice for a light to the peoples.
text.Rotherham: Attend unto me, O my people, And, O, my nation unto me give ear,—For, instruction, from me, shall go forth, And, my justice—for a light of peoples, will I establish:
text.DRB: Hearken unto me, O my people, and give ear to me, O my tribes: for a law shall go forth from me, and my judgment shall rest to be a light of the nations.

text.NRSV: I will bring near my deliverance swiftly, my salvation has gone forth and my arms will rule the peoples; the coastlands wait for me, and for my arm they hope.
text.Rotherham: At hand is my righteousness. Gone forth hath my salvation, And, mine own arms, unto the peoples, shall give justice,—Upon me, Coastlands, shall wait, And for mine arm, shall they hope.
text.DRB: My just one is near at hand, my saviour is gone forth, and my arms shall judge the people: the islands shall look for me, and shall patiently wait for my arm.

text.NRSV: Lift up your eyes to the heavens, and look at the earth beneath; for the heavens will vanish like smoke, the earth will wear out like a garment, and those who live on it will die like gnats; but my salvation will be forever, and my deliverance will never be ended.
text.Rotherham: Lift up—to the heavens—your eyes, And look around to the earth beneath Though, the heavens, like smoke, should have vanished And, the earth, like a garment, should fall to pieces, And, her inhabitants, in like manner, should die, Yet, my salvation, unto times age-abiding, shall continue, And, my righteousness, shall not be broken down.
text.DRB: Lift up your eyes to heaven, and look down to the earth beneath: for the heavens shall vanish like smoke, and the earth shall be worn away like a garment, and the inhabitants thereof shall perish in like manner: but my salvation shall be for ever, and my justice shall not fail.

text.NRSV: Listen to me, you who know righteousness, you people who have my teaching in your hearts; do not fear the reproach of others, and do not be dismayed when they revile you.
text.Rotherham: Hearken unto me ye who discern righteousness, The people in whose heart is my law,—Do not fear the reproach of frail men, Nor at their revilings, be dismayed;
text.DRB: Hearken to me, you that know what is just, my people who have my law in your heart: fear ye not the reproach of men, and be not afraid of their blasphemies.

text.NRSV: For the moth will eat them up like a garment, and the worm will eat them like wool; but my deliverance will be forever, and my salvation to all generations.
text.Rotherham: For like a garment, shall they be eaten of the moth, And like wool, shall they be eaten of the larva; But, my righteousness, unto times age-abiding shall continue, And, my salvation, unto the remotest generation.
text.DRB: For the worm shall eat them up as a garment: and the moth shall consume them as wool: but my salvation shall be for ever, and my justice from generation to generation,

text.NRSV: Awake, awake, put on strength, O arm of the Lord! Awake, as in days of old, the generations of long ago! Was it not you who cut Rahab in pieces, who pierced the dragon?
text.Rotherham: Awake, awake put on strength, O arm of Yahweh, Awake, As in days of old, The generations of bygone ages: Art not thou that which—Hewed down Rahab, Piercing the Crocodile?
text.DRB: Arise, arise, put on strength, O thou arm of the Lord, arise as in the days of old, in the ancient generations. Hast not thou struck the proud one, and wounded the dragon?

text.NRSV: Was it not you who dried up the sea, the waters of the great deep; who made the depths of the sea a way for the redeemed to cross over?
text.Rotherham: Art not thou that which dried up—The Sea, The waters of the mighty Deep,—That which made of the abysses of the sea a road for the passing over of the redeemed?
text.DRB: Hast not thou dried up the sea, the water of the mighty deep, who madest the depth of the sea a way, that the delivered might pass over?

text.NRSV: So the ransomed of the Lord shall return, and come to Zion with singing; everlasting joy shall be upon their heads; they shall obtain joy and gladness, and sorrow and sighing shall flee away.
